Hi JanSz,
I have posted my information below. Please let me know if you need any additional information. Thanks

**My 24 hour routine is as follows:**

-   I wake up at 4AM and just lay in bed until 5 to 5:30AM
-   I take a shower and have breakfast around 6:30AM. Breakfast is usually two eggs and some type of steak, breakfast sausage, turkey or chicken. Occasionally I have a vegetable protein shake in almond milk.
-   I head off to work about 7:15 AM and once at work I am on the computer until 5PM.
-   I arrive at home at 5:15PM and watch a little TV. I then start dinner. I do some house chores and then relax for the rest of the evening. I may get some sun after 6PM for about 20 to 30 minutes.
-   I am in bed at around 10PM. I take some melatonin about 30 minutes before bed, anywhere from 5-8 mgs. I usually fall asleep within 10 minutes. I wake up at around 4AM daily. On rare occasion if I happen to go to bed later, I will wake up later in the morning. Last night I watched the fight. I didn't get home until almost 1AM. I went to bed right away and fell asleep quickly and woke up at almost 7AM.
-   On the weekend, I will have breakfast around 7 to 8 AM. If there is sun I will get some sun exposure at around 8am for about 20-30 minutes. I may also get 15 minutes at around 11AM. I do my shopping for groceries and take care of any pending items. The rest of the day I spend doing housework, washing and ironing clothes. I get on the internet for a couple of hours to do some research . Sunday is mostly a day of rest, but may do some more shopping at the Farmers market.
-   My diet consists of natural beef, chicken, turkey, lamb, offal's, fish. I eat only organic vegetables. I drink only spring water or RO water.
-   In regards to light, I have tried to minimize exposure to artificial light in the evenings. I bought blue blocker glasses recently and have been wearing them at sunset (about 8pm CST). I removed all electrical devices from my bedroom. I turn off the wifi before bedtime and set my cell phone to airplane mode. My bedroom is completely dark.
-   I was eating dinner at around 7:30 to 8 PM daily but have recently starting eating dinner by 6PM.

Notes on myself:

-   I am 58 years of age, 5' 9" in height, 172 lbs
-   Symptoms of a leaky gut
-   Allergies
-   Very dry skin
-   Eye inflammation and gritty eyes in the evenings
-   Inflammation in joints
-   Back hurts often.
-   Can't handle too much sodium, even sea salt.
-   I get startled easily,
-   Super sensitive to perfumes, chemicals, etc
-   Chronic constipation
-   Muscle loss
-   Tire around my waist
-   My arms and legs hurt when I wake up. Arms get cold also.
-   My muscles get sore easily after physical activity.
-   Wake up with a headache after physical activity the day before.
-   If I have starchy food for dinner I wake up with a headache.
-   Extreme hair loss over the last 13 years.
-   Was diagnosed with a mild case of sleep apnea about a year ago
-   I rarely exercise because I normally feel drained.

My health issues started about 13 years ago when I had a large number of amalgam fillings removed by a non-biological dentist who had no idea about safe removal. My health wasn't the greatest before removing my amalgams, but a lot better than after removing the amalgams fillings. If I could go back in time I would have my filling removed. Prior to removing my amalgams about 10 years prior, I was under a loss of mental and physical stress. I was in a terrible marriage, was working in a job I hated, was going to night school and had a daughter who was born with a congenital heart defect and consequently had open heart surgery. During this time my extremities starting getting cold whenever I was in a cold environment and this symptom has continued to this day.
In regards to my low cortisol which I have had for years, according to the individuals I have spoken to, my only option at this points seems to be Hydrocortisone. I am considering this as an option, but would like to correct it in a more natural way if possible. Any comments are appreciated. Thanks

### JanSz — 2015-05-09T20:17:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-163723>

Getting it together.
\------------------------

I have numerous issues, some of which are low adrenal function (daytime saliva cortisol are extremely low, nighttime levels are high), low testosterone, leaky gut, heavy metal toxicity, candida overgrowth, sleep apnea, always tired, body aches, inflammation, frequent headaches, etc.

I live In south Texas. It's a small city named Edinburg. It's about 230 miles south of San Antonio.

I am not feeling well. I am completely wiped out both physically and mentally. I try to rest, but I always wake up at 4am like clockwork and feel just as tired as when I went to bed. What I wouldn't give to get a night of sleep that made me feel rested and energized in the morning.

I have been drinking R/O and spring water for over 15 years. I even cook with purified water.

As for seafood, I rarely eat it because of the mercury. I eat mostly natural chicken, turkey, lamb, beef, and a little pork.

I went to bed at 10:30PM last night and was wide awake by 3:30AM this morning. I look forward to the day I sleep 8 hours in a night. Seven hours sounds pretty good too.

You mentioned that I should eat four hours before bedtime. What if a person's sugar level is possibly dropping in the night and waking them up? I think I may have that issue.

It was recommended that I eat my BAB within 30 minutes of rising. So if I am currently waking up at 3:30AM, I should be eating my BAB by 4AM? Normally I usually lie in bed until 5:30 to 6:00AM, get up and shower then eat around 6:30.

I will get some candles and red lamps and look into the salt lamps. Do the red light bulbs need to be incandescent light bulbs?

Yes I have the f.lux on my home PC and Lap top. I installed in about 6 months ago. It really has helped with the brightness issue. I just installed it on my PC at work yesterday, where I spend about 8 hours on the computer daily.

Currently there is no one in my life. My current health situation and lack of sleep does not put me in a position to be with anyone and give them the quality attention they deserve. My health has cost me a wife and a few girlfriends.

The bottom line is that you should evaluate your situation using
mostly blood tests.
Saliva is useful to check cortisol and melatonin, specially the shape of your rhythm
Urine tests are also useful.

When you would use DHEA and pregnenolone pills,
specially when additionally you would use supplement list build on
results from Spectracell Micronutrient test you can expect to make big progress
in area that some call Adrenal Fatigue.

You should not expect big progress in raising testosterone.
It works that way (sometimes) with ladies, but not with men.
That is, you could expect some testosterone raise as much as lady would,
which in men counts for nothing.

Your testosterone (may) raise when you will start using HCG,
providing
that your testicles are capable of producing additional testosterone.

I mentioned before that your pituitary is suspect (low LH when testosterone is low).
That made me suspect that your GH, growth hormone may also be low.
If GH is low, correcting that deficiency could help a lot (after 6-9+ months of using it).
You would then use Norditropin (rather than making "savings" using other brands.
You would not waste your good will and energy, time and $$ on peptides.

/
Make sure that you follow dr Kruse advice.

/

### JanSz — 2015-05-12T18:32:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-163980>

The biggest problem you have is with light.
Light and non-native EMF.

Thinking of cortisol.
If you implement what we discussed so far, I hope that you end up with plenty of DHEAs and Cortisol,
but
that will be on average on 24hr basis.

Next problem is to redistribute all that cortisol properly over various demands throughout the day.
To do that there is basically no other way except that you need to be exposed to proper light frequency at the right time of the day.
We are talking about your sleep problems (and your energy).

One of the most important items is that you should keep your schedule constant, 24/7 throughout the days/weeks/months.
Do not change your light exposure between workdays and weekends.
I know it is hard to implement.
Likely most important are, say, first two hours of the day.
When you get up (in your completely dark) bedroom
you need to exposure you face to sun.
Luckily you work on first shift, people working night shifts are in completely hopeless situation.
Since you are getting up before sunrise, you should get bright light.
Either special light or for a time being get as much bright lights as you can. (500-1000W).
Hopefully ceiling of your bedroom is painted white. Direct that light on the ceiling, do not look directly at light.

Next problem with lighting is after sundown.
You do not want much light then.
You do not want any blue light then (tv, phones, monitors).
For lighting you may want to use low wattage red bulbs that you can get in local Home Depot.
When you go to bedroom, all lights are out.

.........
blue light=big problem
more precisely,
when living completely outdoor, we are exposed to various amounts of blue light. That amount varies when the sun rays have to go thru various thickness of atmosphere.
Most of blue light makes thru within 10AM-2PM
When you compare that amount of blue light with exposure from tv, monitors, telephones, it turns out that we are getting from technology not only
light are the wrong time
but
also too much overall

One of the most important items is that you should keep your schedule constant, 24/7 throughout the days/weeks/months. Do not change your light exposure between workdays and weekends. I know it is hard to implement. Likely most important are, say, first two hours of the day.
When you get up (in your completely dark) bedroom you need to exposure you face to sun.
Luckily you work on first shift, people working night shifts are in completely hopeless situation.
Since you are getting up before sunrise, you should get bright light. Either special light or for a time being get as much bright lights as you can. (500-1000W). Hopefully ceiling of your bedroom is painted white. Direct that light on the ceiling, do not look directly at light.

Next problem with lighting is after sundown. You do not want much light then. You do not want any blue light then (tv, phones, monitors). For lighting you may want to use low wattage red bulbs that you can get in local Home Depot. When you go to bedroom, all lights are out.

**So I eliminate the use my PC, TV and cell phone after sunset? Even with blue blockers?

Thanks and best regards.**

**My quality of sleep must be terrible, because even if I sleep for 6 hours straight, which is rare, I wake up feeling drained.
\================================================**

We sleep in cycles.

Make it your project to eliminate alarm clock.
For example set it 1/2 to 1 hr latter, when is the very latest time to get up.

When you get up naturally, you do that on the end of sleep cycle.
You should feel more rested if you start your day that way.

Alarm clock will always wake you up in the middle of sleep cycle.
It ruins quality of sleep.

You said: "I live In south Texas. It's a small city named Edinburg. It's about 230 miles south of San Antonio."
How long are you living there? Have you always lived at the same latitude.
How about your Mom & Dad?
I am mostly concerned with huge latitude changes.

For example, people moving from lover states to Alaska often have disruption in their Circadian Rhythm.

When I moved from Poland (50.2833° N) to New Jersey (40.8596° N)
Ten degrees have not created any problems for me.
But, when I worked for little over a year in Brazil (Latitude= -23.00773, Longitude= -44.4588)
at first I was excited, have not paid much attention but 3-4 months into it I was having problems with light.
Luckily it was not major situation at the time. Do not know if I would get used to living there permanently, because life was good there, otherwise.
Relatively speaking I have made my situation somewhat better:
no major change to time zones (like Poland vs NJ, 6 hrs
was getting up at about sunrise (no change)
was getting much more and much stronger light exposure, construction work, little in the office, lots outdoor time on a hot sun (every day)
remote jungle area ---> no EMF
but almost constant day/nigh length (12hr/12hr) was uncomfortable to me.

Can you relate to that?
[http://globalenergyobservatory.org/geoid/2121](http://globalenergyobservatory.org/geoid/2121)

### JanSz — 2015-05-18T18:17:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-164614>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/are-your-blue-blockers-working.13985/)
****[PaulG](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/paulg.1838/)****

**\=============================**
**Are your blue blockers working?**
[http://www.readingglassesetc.com/blog/common-misconceptions-about-blue-light-and-blue-block-lenses/](http://www.readingglassesetc.com/blog/common-misconceptions-about-blue-light-and-blue-block-lenses/)
Common Misconceptions About Blue Light and Blue Block Lenses

**Common Misconception #1 - All Blue Light Is Harmful.**

Not true. Some blue light is necessary for good vision.

_"**Not all blue light is bad.** The labeled blue-turquoise light range, which is from **495 nm to 465 nm,** is essential to our vision, the function of our pupillary reflex, and in general to human health. It also helps to regulate our Circadian sleep/wake cycle.11 So blue light in general can have healthy affects on vision as well as the body, and it is this blue-turquoise light that tends to have these beneficial effects. **Inadequate light exposure means inadequate blue-turquoise light, which can throw off our Circadian biological clock and our sleep/wake cycle.** So this blue-turquoise light really plays a vital role in the general health of the individual."_ - Dr Ronald Melton, OD from [The Lowdown on Blue Light: Good vs Bad and It's Connection to AMD](http://www.reviewofoptometry.com/continuing_education/tabviewtest/lessonid/109744/); Review of Optometry **February 2014**
\---------------------------------------------------------------
**UV400 lenses filter 400-280nm** (UV-A and UV-B). Visible light is 780-380nm.

\---------------------------------------------------------------

The idea is that as long as you are not color blind, you view the chart through your blue block lenses and both spectrums should look the same.

////////////////////////////////////////

I could use dr Jack Kruse and PaulG review here:
To reset your circadian Rhythm you should inspect and adjust your exposure to:
blue-turquoise light range, which is from 495 nm to 465 nm,
You want to be exposed to it from sunrise to sunset (say minus 2-3 hrs before sunset)
You want it to be all absent sunset to sunrise

How to achieve that:
a. use daylight (no glasses, no windows, no windshields) as much as possible.Look at sun (closed eylids).
b. use Philips GoLITE BLU Energy Light, Rechargeable w/Travel Pouch (post #55)

After sundown use red lights (and I think also NIR and IR-A (Near Infrared)(post #51).
Or more expensive LED, 650nm visible red, 850nm nearly invisible IR, post #11.
Above is likely somewhat less important, as long as you stay away from blue-turquoise light range, which is from 495 nm to 465 nm, (using eye glasses blocking them).

**So, lets look around and try to find out eyeglasses that block (blue-turquoise light range 495 nm to 465 nm)**
because it will be hard to eliminate tv, cell phone, computer monitors (but use flux.com etc)(limit use of technology)

may have been somewhat off the mark or off the intended goal.

What is UV400? ------>>UV400 lenses filter 400-280nm (UV-A and UV-B)
common-misconceptions\-about-blue-light-and-blue-block-lenses

### PaulG — 2015-05-19T07:07:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-164664>

The above article is focused on damage to the retina via exposure to 400-280nm (UV-A and UV-B) not on the multifarious effects of bluelight after sundown on circadian biology.

Any blue light (500-381nm) after sundown can cause suppression of melatonin but Short-wavelength light is maximally effective at suppressing melatonin (peak sensitivity z 460 nm)
See [Light level and duration of exposure determine the impact of self-luminous tablets on melatonin suppression](https://www.gwern.net/docs/melatonin/2012-wood.pdf)

but also light in the yellow and green spectrum can have an effect.

and [http://www.gunnars.com/the-influenc...neal-melatonin-content-in-the-syrian-hamster/](http://www.gunnars.com/the-influence-of-different-light-spectra-on-the-suppression-of-pineal-melatonin-content-in-the-syrian-hamster/)

> The purpose of this study was to test the capacity of different visible wavelengths of light to suppress nocturnal levels of pineal melatonin in hamsters. It was found that the visible wavelengths vary in their ability to perturb pineal melatonin. During the period of peak pineal melatonin production, animals were exposed to fluorescent light sources having half-peak bandwidths of 339–371 nm (near-ultraviolet), 435–500 nm (blue), 510–550 nm (green), 558–636 nm (yellow) and 653–668 nm (red). In each experiment, animals were exposed to equal irradiances of each light source. The different irradiances used were 0.928, 0.200, 0.186, 0.074 and 0.019 μW/cm2. **The resultant data demonstrated that blue fluorescent light was the most efficient in suppressing pineal melatonin. Green fluorescent light was found to be the next most efficient light for inhibiting pineal melatonin followed by yellow fluorescent light. Near-ultraviolet and red light were the least capable of suppressing pineal melatonin**. These observations suggest that the retinal photopigment responsible for mediating the pineal gland’s response to light in the hamster may be either rhodopsin or another blue-sensitive chromophore.

[See also INFLUENCE OF NEAR-ULTRAVIOLET RADIATION ON REPRODUCTIVE AND IMMUNOLOGICAL DEVELOPMENT IN JUVENILE MALE SIBERIAN HAMSTERS](http://jeb.biologists.org/content/204/14/2535.full.pdf)

If you can change your schedule somehow so that you walk or hike (outdoors) for 1hr in the AM (6-7am) and 1hr in the PM (5-7pm) I think that would help. Red Light Therapy would also help. See [https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/1005029886176572/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/permalink/1005029886176572/)

Hello JanSz, Below is my sleep log for the last week:

Sleep Log, for the last week:

**Wednesday (May 5, 2015):** Went to bed at 10PM. I woke up at 2:50AM. Could not fall back to sleep. Got out of bad at 4:30AM
**Thursday:** went to bed at 10PM, I woke up at 3AM. Got out of bed at 5AM
**Friday:** went to bed at 10PM, I woke up at 4AM. I got up at 5AM.
**Saturday**: I was very tired after lunch and went to lie down for a few minutes and slept for around an hour. It’s rare when I sleep during the day. I limited my exposure to artificial blue light during the day. Was in darkness the last couple of hours before bed, except a 15W red light. I was very sleepy by 9PM and went to bed by 9:30PM. I got up twice to urinate and went back to sleep both times. The second time I woke up it was 3AM. I urinated quite a bit both times. I woke up for the 3rd time at 4:30AM and got out of bed at 5AM
**Sunday**: I was very tired by 9PM. I went to bed at 9:30PM and woke up to go to the bathroom at least once and woke up and got out of bed at 5:45AM.
**Monday**: Went to bed at 9:30PM. I woke up once to urinate and then went back to sleep. Woke up a second time at 4:50AM. I stayed in bed until around 5:30AM then got up.
**Tuesday**: Went to bed at 10PM. I couldn’t fall sleep. The only change I made today from my normal routine was that I took pregnenolone in the morning. This was the first time I took pregnenolone. It did make me feel more energetic in the afternoon, but gave me insomnia at night and this morning I felt drained more than ever.

**Note**: I had been taking 8mg of melatonin about 30 minutes before going to bed. Starting last Friday night, I started taking 10mg of melatonin. I take 5mg, 30 minutes before bed then 5mg of time release melatonin at bed time. I fall asleep within minutes of laying down. Thanks

### PaulG — 2015-05-21T06:11:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-164885>

Hi Eddie

Long term you want to normalize circadian rhythms - supplementing with super high amounts of Melatonin may not be helping in this long term goal. You need to increase production of melatonin naturally which would include:

-   Get a sleep study done to rule out Sleep Apnea
-   Getting as much sunshine/light during the day (Can increase night-time Melatonin production by up to 50%). Though limit exposure at high UV periods ([In Texas 11-3pm](http://uv.willyweather.com/tx/galveston-county/galveston.html))
-   Limiting evening light (Try NO-blue light after sundown eg turn on only low-level red-lights and read in bed for 2hrs - go to bed at 8pm) - see how you feel.
-   No stimulants e.g Caffeine
-   [Rewild yourself](http://www.danielvitalis.com/rewild-yourself-magazine/) and adopt [MoveNat](https://www.movnat.com/) principles to become less of a [human zoo animal](http://www.rewildthyself.com/what-is-rewilding/)
-   No Carbs after 3pm
-   See a qualified endocrinologist/horrmone specialist
-   Review some of the posts here on Melatonin. See [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/1609332/&q=melatonin&o=date&c\[title\_only\]=1](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/1609332/&q=melatonin&o=date&c[title_only]=1)
-   Check out Dan Pardi
    -   [http://sigmanutrition.com/episode38/](http://sigmanutrition.com/episode38/)
    -

Hope that helps

I think that during day time
wearing glasses, either blue blockers or just dark glasses is misunderstanding, specially because you tested and have low cortisol.

You need high intensity light during day (to make cortisol, to suppress melatonin)
You need total darkness after sundown (to make melatonin, to suppress cortisol).
\------
Using blue-blockers in the evening is compromise that we use because we invented technology.
Preferably, you could use only red light in the evening for lighting.
As @[PaulG](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1838/) said, other than red light, all other frequencies will suppress melatonin. Just the blue is worst in doing it.
Actually the "blue blockers" were produced to remedy another problem, and they block only
UV400--->400-280nm
the big offender at night if our big friend during day,
that is 495 nm to 465 nm
and those "blue blockers" are not filtering it out.
\-------------

During day time use... they are portable, use it from the moment you get off bed till about noon

\==========================================================

Please arrange for prescription for blood testing at LabCorp.
Either from doctor of from re-seler.

### Jill1 — 2015-05-22T03:14:18+0300

> Hi Jill, What does VIP stand for? How does one determine if they have a biotoxin Infection? Thanks for your comments.

VIP is a hormone in your body. Check out Dr Shoemaker's website for more info on biotoxins. They list labs & doctors who treat it. Bugs are bad news. For Example, Microbes create nagalase to attach to the vitamin D3 receptor and block it. No matter how much D3 is present, the cells don’t wake up. You can stand in the sun & still have D levels that are not working - even with normal lab values (over 50) If microbes are manipulating you, nagalase will be high. Anything over 0.6 is pathological. 3.5 and above is pretty bad.

### Eddie Garza — 2015-05-25T20:19:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-165317>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=164885)
>
> Hi Eddie
>
> Long term you want to normalize circadian rhythms - supplementing with super high amounts of Melatonin may not be helping in this long term goal. You need to increase production of melatonin naturally which would include:
>
> -   Get a sleep study done to rule out Sleep Apnea. **I did have one done about a year and a half ago. I was diagnosed with a case of mild sleep apnea. I bought the CPAP machine, but I just can't sleep with it. I did try the oral device and used it for a few months, but I didn't feel and improvement on how I felt so I stopped using it.**
> -   Getting as much sunshine/light during the day (Can increase night-time Melatonin production by up to 50%). Though limit exposure at high UV periods ([In Texas 11-3pm](http://uv.willyweather.com/tx/galveston-county/galveston.html)). **I have been getting more sunshine when I can, but we've had so many cloudy rainy days it's been difficult. Weekends is when I have the best opportunity to get sun. M-F is difficult due to my work schedule. I can get sun when I get home at 6pm. Would getting sun that late benefit me?**
> -   Limiting evening light (Try NO-blue light after sundown eg turn on only low-level red-lights and read in bed for 2hrs - go to bed at 8pm) - see how you feel. **If I wear my blue blockers can I use the computer after sundown?**
> -   No stimulants e.g Caffeine. **Done.**
> -   [Rewild yourself](http://www.danielvitalis.com/rewild-yourself-magazine/) and adopt [MoveNat](https://www.movnat.com/) principles to become less of a [human zoo animal](http://www.rewildthyself.com/what-is-rewilding/). **I will follow up on this. Thanks**
> -   No Carbs after 3pm. **I can do this, but what is the purpose for no carbs after 3pm?**
> -   See a qualified endocrinologist/horrmone specialist. **I have and all my test results are in the "normal range". My testosterone is on the extreme low end of the normal range.**
> -   Review some of the posts here on Melatonin. See [http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/1609332/&q=melatonin&o=date&c\[title\_only\]=1](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?search/1609332/&q=melatonin&o=date&c[title_only]=1) **Thanks!**
> -   Check out Dan Pardi.
>     -   [http://sigmanutrition.com/episode38/](http://sigmanutrition.com/episode38/)
>     -   **I watched it today and it is a very interesting presentation. I definitely need to get more of those sun rays daily and like you mentioned, and so has JanSz, no blue lights after sunset.**
>
> Hope that helps. **Yes it will and thank you.**

Hi Paul, Thank you for your post. I was rereading all the posts and I thought I had replied to your, but I guess I hadn't. My apologies. I have provided replies to your comments above in bold font. Thank you very much for your contributions. Best regards.

### JanSz — 2015-05-26T02:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-165351>

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=165315)
>
> Hi JanSz,
> I didn't find anything with the name Fatty Acid profile. I checked 10 online labs. Life Extension does offer this test: [http://www.lifeextension.com/Vitamins-Supplements/itemLCOMEGA/Omega-Check](http://www.lifeextension.com/Vitamins-Supplements/itemLCOMEGA/Omega-Check)
> Thanks.

I do not think that test is beneficial.
LEF.org is very good company and provides lost of good information.
Unfortunately it sells fish oils and likely it is watching $$ that come from those sales.
There is a lots of hype about omega3 and omega6.
There is a whole industry selling fish oils and testing them in a way that promotes sales.
Tests are made is such a way that the bottom line is that they advice you to buy fish or krill oil.
It resembles really cholesterol testing in a way that promotes sales of statins.

You want test that checks individual fatty acids and give status of each one.
Saying omega3 or omega6 misses the boat.
Dr Kruse want us to have lots of DHA.
But one cannot be blind to what that DHA comes with.
It comes with excessive amounts of EPA that result is suppression of all omega6, and specially AA.
That really goes not only for fish or krill oils.
Seafood also have excessive EPA, no big surprise, that is where fish oil comes from.
Practically I do not see other way to sort this out except by blood testing.
Each case is unique.
Very few people do this type testing.
I did it numerous times and I have found my EPA super high and AA super low, just like expected.

///
Consider asking your local doctor to write you script just for

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

////////////

### JanSz — 2015-05-26T16:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-165422>

Copy the ICD-9 codes (circled) on the bottom of the script.
That may help your doctor with writing script for the Fatty Acids analysis.
With those codes insurance should pay for that test.
\=========================================

Look also at the table on the bottom.
Column F gives %Status.
Mid range = 0 (zero) (desirable condition)
(we may want to push up DHA because of dr Kruse, but no more than to the top range (+25%), if it is more than that it would start distorting other fatty acids)

Columns G, H, I, J, K, L
give %status (col F) in 25% steps
one wants to place within col I & K
that is +-25% of the mid range (green)

I got idea about that table following dr Patricia Kane analysis
as shown in her book "The BodyBio Detoxx Book"
You can see some of her examples here:
[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)

On that tests I have %Status:

+89.09% too much DHA
+338.37% too much EPA
\-39.69% not enough AA

### JanSz — 2015-05-28T18:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-165694>

//
zeitgeber
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zeitgeber](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zeitgeber)
A **zeitgeber** is any external or environmental cue that entrains, or synchronizes, an organism's [biological rhythms](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Circadian_rhythm) to the Earth's 24-hour light/dark cycle and 12 month cycle

Common examples of zeitgebers

-   Light
-   Temperature
-   Social interactions
-   Pharmacological manipulation
-   Exercise
-   Eating/drinking patterns

//
Almost every disease known to modern medicine is tied to **altered ubiquination processing.**
This is why circadian timing is the most critical aspect of wellness.
Health and wellness starts with cyclic and structural cycles **and not with food!**
\===============================

Changes in light is how we release our pituitary hormones.
**This means light and timing are fundamentally linked to proper signaling in the human brain.**
If signaling is awry, what you eat will matter little,
but when you eat really matter in a big way.

Clothes block sulfation of D3 and cholesterol
living at a higher altitude ----->> depression and suicide
.
[](http://imageshack.com/f/jlimage118bj)

Example:
When people like you go to (good) doctor,
he tells them that
they have:
weak adrenals that need support (about xx minerals, vitamins, other)
thyroid system is not processing correctly (another yy minerals, vitamins, other)
steroid hormones do not convert correctly (another zz minerals, vitamins, other)
fatty acids are not converting correctly, delta 6, delta 5 desaturases, elongase do not work as they should(lets try another ww minerals, vitamins, etc)

Now you have a list of 30 items that you can name and 5 that are proprietary.
Doctor have no idea what you actually need. He have 15 years of trial and error in front of him.
\==============
Micronutrient test will tell you directly what you have and what you missing.

Now your first choice is to adjust diet,
second choice is to use supplementation.
6 - 12 months latter you can do next test to see how your intervention works.

\=========

### JanSz — 2015-06-04T17:27:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-166552>

You may see sometimes ratio 4:1 being mentioned in regard to fatty acids.
Sometimes even more fuzzy, it is called Omega6/Omega3 ratio.
Some people are curious and want to know what it is actually, how it came about and how to use it.
\==============
[http://www.direct-ms.org/pdf/NutritionFats/Yehuda Omega 3 6 ratio.pdf](http://www.direct-ms.org/pdf/NutritionFats/Yehuda%20Omega%203%206%20ratio.pdf)
**Shlomo Yehuda**
came to this conclusion by feeding his rats with highly purified linoleic and alpha-linolenic acids.
\======================
Then Patricia Kane clarify:
[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/BodyBioBulletin-4to1Oil.pdf)
The genius level of Yehuda et al’s
work, and its contribution to humanity, is of such import to
the world, to warrant consideration of a Nobel Prize.

But --- remember --- there are two levels of EFAs, and the
4:1 oil addresses only the lower level, LA and ALA, not
the higher --- the HUFAs. At the present time, **without a
Red Blood Cell Fatty Acid analysis**, there is no way of
accurately assessing how much HUFAs, AA, GLA, EPA,
or DHA, the body needs.
\===========================
I add to above that eating 4:1 does not mean that you expect to get 4:1 on tests.
\===========================

Going back to your severe sleep problem, in his article Shlomo Yehuda writes:

There is evidence that prostaglandin D2 has a **profound beneficial effect on
sleep.**
On the other hand, other prostaglandins enhance CRF (corticotropin-
releasing factor) activity, **which enhances wakefulness.**
CRF, in turn, induces the release of prostaglandins. Prostaglandins also enhance TRH (thyrotropin-releasing hormone) release, and stimulate the dopaminergic and noradrenergic
receptor activity, while beta-endorphin inhibits the prostaglandin synthesis
\[11\]. Very few studies examine the effect of different ratios on production
and/or activity of various prostaglandins.

\=============================

I post above to tell you that you should make an effort to get your fatty acid analysis done as I mentioned in earlier post.

///////

### Eddie Garza — 2015-06-10T17:43:15+0300

Hi Jan, I hope all is well. Just wanted to provide an update. I seem to be sleeping a little better. Previously I had been waking up at 3 or 4 AM daily. The last four days my bed time has been from 10 to 10:30PM. I slept until 7AM on Saturday, 6AM on Sunday, 5Am on Monday, and until 6AM last night. I still don't have the energy in the morning. I seem to have feel my best in the evenings. I remember submitting a question to Dr. Lam around 5 months ago and gave him my symptoms and he said I had a reverse cortisol curve. Hopefully getting my circadian rhythm balanced will adjust this. I listened to the Dan Pardi Video that was recommended on this thread and he mentions that.

I've also been reading Dr. Kruse' articles on Ubiquitination. I haven't read all the articles yet, but the following items stood out in my early reading:

-   Controls maintaining muscle mass
-   Circadian rhythm
-   Embrace cold as much as possible
-   Live at sea level
-   Sunlight is vital: drives the circadian signals of the brain
-   Suprachiasmatic nucleus: Biological clock in our brain for sleep and wakefulness
-   DHA is important for the eye
-   Iodine prevents DHA oxidation
-   Low melatonin and Vitamin D and high ubiquination leads to disease generation
-   Blue light destroys DHA in cells

Best regards.
Eddie

### JanSz — 2015-06-20T17:49:45+0300

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=169033)
>
> I have been feeling so drained for the last few weeks. I think it began when I started the pregnenolone/dhea. I've been contemplating taking a physiologic dose of HC.

Pregnenolone provides more natural distribution of hormones.
When it works, cortisol is produced and delivered based on body signals in just in time fashion.
Hydrocortisone is an end product that acts immediately.
All natural regulation is by-passed. To work, relatively precise timing is required, day in, day out.

Hydrocortisone is good in survival situation.
Pregnenolone (when works) provide profile resembling natural.
\=========================

page 192
Hydrocortisone dose for men
sedentary 20-60mg/day
stress/infection 1.5-3 of the normal dose
dose schedule, 2 to 4 times per day, mainly morning and midday
\-----
example of 4x/day dosing
15mg (6AM)+ 10mg(11AM)+5mg(3PM)+5mg(7PM) (total 35mg/day)

### JanSz — 2015-06-26T01:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-169597>

Look at my script on the picture below.
Look at yellow sticker.

Ask doctor to write line #23 on script
and below it all circled numbers.

I did that today about 1:00 PM
at 1:30PM had blood drawn at LabCorp.
You have to be fasting 12hrs and do not eat specially high fat food day or two before.

Yes, you need doctor who wants to help you,
and not someone who must prove to you how important he is.

Doc I went to takes everybody, accepts all insurances, welfare cases etc.
Recently he came to my home with two of his nurses when my wife was sick and had a hard time getting of the bed.
He was offering to see my Mother in my home, but she always managed to make to his office.
My Mom lived with us her last 25 years, after my Father died in Poland.
She died 3/22/2005, 95y old. Only after she died I realized how much I loved her.

Overall cost from my wallet pov is $0.00.

Hi Jan, In regards to my high Estrone levels, I read that increased estrone levels may be associated with increased levels of circulating androgens and their subsequent peripheral conversion. Does that mean that my testosterone could be going upwards from taking pregnenolone, DHEA and other supplements I am taking to help with T? Thanks

### JanSz — 2015-08-04T17:28:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-173917>

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=173914)
>
> Hi Jan, In regards to my high Estrone levels, I read that increased estrone levels may be associated with increased levels of circulating androgens and their subsequent peripheral conversion. Does that mean that my testosterone could be going upwards from taking pregnenolone, DHEA and other supplements I am taking to help with T? Thanks

This is theoretical possibility.
I would not count much on dhea supplementation increasing testosterone level in man. Testosterone may indeed increase tiny bit, enough to make difference for woman.

It may eventually increase E1 estrone via androstenedione, that must be monitored.

When manipulating hormones we need to monitor progress and adjust as need.

Your testosterone level is mostly dependent on:

1-- ability of your testicles to produce testosterone when stimulated (still unknown)(that is one of the reasons why I suggested using HCG first, before any testosterone supplementation).
Time is passing by, you should work on getting HCG, either from your doc or likely easier, and cheaper via internet.

2-- stimulating signal, mostly LH for testosterone and FSH for sperm

Your LH is known to be low.
That indicate that pituitary is not working too well.

FSH 3.4 RR 1.6-8.0 mIU/mL
LH 1.8, RR 1.5-9.3 mIU/mL
Testosterone 308, RR 241-827 ng/dL

.

Hi Jan, my high estrone results got me a bit concerned. This is the first time I've had it tested. I found the following protocol for flushing estrogen from the body. It make a lot of sense. I've ordered the items that are included in the protocol and will proceed once I receive them. From what I've read, high estrogen lowers testosterone so maybe my T levels will benefit. It surely can't hurt. Thanks

**3-Step Estrogen Flush Protocol

Step 1: DIM

The protocol starts with a natural compound called 3,3′-Diindolylmethane (DIM).**
It helps the liver to metabolize estrogen molecules away from the body. Secondly, it converts strong estrogen molecules into their less potent forms
**Step 2: Methylator

The protocol continues with** [betaine](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B00020ID0W/?tag=anabmen-20) and/or [choline](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B004JO4EFU/?tag=anabmen-20)**, which are both strong methylators in the human body.**

By design your estrogen molecules are all missing one methyl group, and this means that your liver can’t chelate them out of the body. In other words they’re stuck inside of you… The methyl groups will then “complete” the empty spots in the estrogen molecules, which makes it possible for your liver to chelate them out of the body
**
Step 3: Calcium-D-Glucarate**

Calcium-D-Glucarate works by binding into those already exiting estrogen molecules. It makes it easier for the molecules to exit the body, so that they won’t get reabsorbed back into the body via the intestines.
**
Summary: Take DIM or eat plenty of crusiferous vegetables – Wait 10 minutes – Take choline and/or betaine or eat plenty of raw eggs and/or beets – Wait 10 minutes – Take some Calcium-D-Glucarate or eat plenty of dark berries to finish of the protocol.**

because
we have to figure what type testosterone you need.
Eventually you need the one that will keep you with adequate DHT
so your penis could work optimally.

\------------------------------------------------

### JanSz — 2015-08-11T20:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-174432>

> [Eddie Garza said:](/goto/post?id=174398)
>
> Thanks JanSz. Is Andro Gel something worth using?

Using any type of testosterone supplementation will suppress LH & FSH,
as consequence your testicles and scrotum will shrink to nothing.
I have been thru this, trying to save you this experience.

At this time we know that your pituitary is slow.
Properly working pituitary would have keept your LH & FSH higher then your test results indicate.
Hopefully that would have caused your testicles to produce more testosterone then you have now.

I think that you should not be deprived from trying this option.
Adding HCG (only) would clarify your status.

You are not loosing anything but may find out that just HCG maybe all you need.

Assuming that HCG only is not enough,
I think, that you should keep HCG and add testosterone.

I suggest that it will be injectable testosterone enanthare or cypionate, 200mg/mL
Weekly dose divided in half and injected at the same time as HCG, twice/week.
You would aim at TT=TotalTestosterone to be within 600-1000ng/dL
Other than proper blood tests, main indicators would be erection any breast sensitivity and blood hemoglobin.

Main driver of good erections are optimal levels of DHT and E2.
Some minimum level of testosterone is also need, but not as much as is usually thought of.
Also one can easily overdo on DHT when using transdermal testosterone, specially that it is rarely tested.

Among people who are low on TotalTestosterone,
few people can get help using HCG,
most people can get optimal using HCG and injectable testosterone,
few people need HCG and transdermal testosterone.

I currently use HCG, injectable testosterone and minimal amounts of transdermal T.

////
When starting on self injections, it is good idea to:
use very small needle
inject around navel.

.

### JanSz — 2015-08-14T18:34:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-174672>

On the picture below, top panel contains all your Fatty acid analysis results, presented in the format that try to replicate dr Patricia Kane analysis.
At this time I do not know how to brake them into (burn it), (build it), (balance it), (indexes) categories.
[http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html](http://www.bodybio-wellness.com/report-fatty-acid.html)
The first three may be somewhat guessed, but I would appreciate help with the Index category.

Middle panel contain yours omega3 and omega6

Bottom panel contain omega3 and omega6 with assumption that all are in mid range, the assumed ideal value.
This assumption follows Patricia Kane approach to this type evaluation.
I may change that if new information becomes available.

Spreadsheet %Status
Whole range=100%
ideal=mid-range
acceptable=+-25% (green)
excess or deficiency of > or < 25% (yellow)
excess or deficiency of > or < 50% (red)

Note that:
#1---excess of any kind of omega3 will suppress Omega6, specially AA, Arachidonic Acid (which is critically important)
#2---excess of Omega6 WILL NOT suppress omega3
#3---LA-Omega6 =(parent oil) all other Omega6 are down-metabolites of LA
#4---ALA-Omega3 =(parent oil) all other Omega3 are down-metabolites of ALA
#5---it is critical to have sufficient amount of AA, Arachidonic Acid
#6---metabolization of fatty acids happens with assistance of desaturases and elongases, they in turn depend heavily on micronutrients. Status of those micronutrients can be evaluated on a wholesale basis using Spectracel test.

About beneficial Fatty Acid 4:1 ratio
About Omega6/Omega3 beneficial 4:1 ratio
I think that we should seek information
but at this time my understanding is that those ratios are **meant for desirable diet and not for blood test result.**
Additionally it is not about some general unspecified Omega3 or Omega6, but specifically about **eating** **four parts of LA to one part of ALA**
LA = Linoleic Acid, C18:2w6
ALA = a-Linolenic Acid, C18 : 3 w3
**That 4:1 ratio was found to be beneficial by Shlomo Yehuda.**
[http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/Remarkable-FattyAcid.pdf](http://www.bodybio.com/BodyBio/docs/Remarkable-FattyAcid.pdf)

\------------------------------------------------------------------------
You have
ideal ALA
almost ideal DHA
missing half of EPA & DPA
\--
too much LA
missing half of the rest of omega6

I suggest that you do not change your diet much,
possibly add 6 whole eggs/week hoping to get some direct AA
and
add one can of sardines or one small salmon steak/week hoping to nudge up EPA.

You may be supplying sufficient amount of LA just not converting it as you should (missing micronutrients).
Speed up your Spectracell test to find out.
After you make corrections per Spectracell test (12 months, another testing at 6 months and 12 months), if you are still not converting properly, you will have to lessen on dietary LA and use GLA supplementation, (Evening Primrose oil). Possibly also supplemental AA.

\===========================

### JanSz — 2015-08-14T21:27:14+0300

Eduardo Garza
**Repletion Suggestions**
1\. Vitamin B1 (Thiamin) 50 mg daily

2\. Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin) 300 mcg daily **(methylcobalamin or adenosylcobalamin)**

3\. Serine 1500 mg daily of L-serine
Take 30 minutes prior to protein intake.

4\. Glutamine 1000 mg t.i.d. (3000 mg daily) Take 30 minutes prior to protein intake.

5\. Glucose-Insulin Interaction **Replace intake of foods with high glycemic index** (sugar, white flour) with whole foods (fruit, vegetables, whole grains, legumes). If chromium deficient, please see repletion for chromium.

6\. Chromium 200 mcg daily of chromium nicotinate or glycinate for 90 days

7\. Glutathione 600 mg t.i.d. (1800 mg daily) of N-Acetylcysteine (NAC). Reduce to
1200 mg daily after 3 months and **retest after 6 months.** Take each dose
with a meal

Please note: Supplementation is usually required for **four to six months to effect the repletion of a functional deficiency in lymphocytes.**

These are sub-optimal choices and this leads to altered cortisol and overall hormone panels. All these changes lead to a lack of catalase in cells and altered UV light light release in cells (too much) and all these sequential changes fuel this low fertility and a low cortisol. All glass block UV.

then after sundown you are adding blue and green that should not be there, that confuses circadian signaling even more.

Ultraviolet A UVA **320 – 400nm**
Long wave, **black light**, not absorbed by the ozone layer
\--------------------------

(That may have been either mistake or accounting for possible difference in light content between equatorial region and sun light we have in northern hemisphere, north of Northen Tropic, Tropic of Cancer.

\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The central retinal-SCN-hypothalamic-pituitary axis needs 290-415 nm light exposure in the AM to function properly. This is why your hormones from the anterior pituitary are almost exclusively released between 6-10 AM as I laid out in the Cold Thermogenesis 7 blog post years ago. This is also why I been telling my members **as soon as they rise they need to go look in the direction of the rising sun _and basking their skin_ in it for period of time as soon as they rise. I also warned them not do it behind glass, plastic, or glasses, ever.** Now you know why I said it.
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Then I thought about Feymann and what he did, and why he did it. I realize immediately, this long standing meme exists publicly and in medicine too. I realized this meme was false physically, yet it is still present today. Full spectrum sunlight, natural sunlight contain 290-400 nm light. All life evolved with this light on earth. How could it be bad? I then found out almost all manufactured lenses of any kind back from 1930 until today are made so they block all UV frequencies of light. The reason for this fear is the result of a general lack of understanding in the differences of the relative intensities of the near or long wavelengths UV light and how our atmosphere allows 290 and above in regular sunlight every morning. The confusion exists because there is far and short wavelength UV that appears in natural sunlight at the surface of Earth. **Day light has shorter wavelength UV in 290-380 range, where twilight has the 381-500 range blue light.** I found out that these frequencies of light interact with different proteins in our eye to do different things. The atmosphere filters and stops virtually all of the far short wave (UVA), except for trace amounts. Natural sunlight, however, allows quite a bit of near long wavelength UV light.

What are the human daytime photosynthetic chemicals that full spectrum sunlight generates? Vitamin D3, histamine, cortisol, and sulfhydryl groups added to cholesterol in the skin I mentioned in Tensegrity 7 blog post.

What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally? UV light lowers adrenalin the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system while its photons re zip collagen that cortisol release at 4AM began to unwind to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep. When we are missing UV and/or full spectrum sunlight in someone who is chronically stressed, sleep can be induced. Think about your childhood when you spent the day at the beach and got a sunburn. This induces sleep. The redness comes not from a burn but from the increase blood flow to the skin to absorb the UV light. Deep sunburns can result excessive sun or thin skin or a poor adaptation to seasonal light due clothing or sunblock use in strong light cycles.

This sleep is a sign of how bad your daily environment really is. UV and IR AM light normally lowers aberrant steroids (CT 7) and nucleic acids in keratinocytes without any liberation of heat on the skin's surface. This helps preventing aberrant optical scattering of cell signaling below (non linear optics). The epidermis has no nuclear DNA because as we rise to the surface DNA is exposed to a cells interior as the cell dies and DNA absorbs all UV light frequencies as a free chemical within the epidermis. It acts as a natural sun protectant or sun blocker. The more photodegredation we sense the more skin cells show up in the epidermis and the skin thickens in summer months. Melanin also absorbs all UV frequencies to serve as a storage protein for the power of UV light to give to other atoms and molecules in the skin. In this way your skin acts like a crystal.

Members who listened to the 2015 Sept got this implicitly.

### JanSz — 2015-09-07T15:51:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-176659>

[http://www.lifestylelaboratory.com/articles/ott/effects-tinted-light.html](http://www.lifestylelaboratory.com/articles/ott/effects-tinted-light.html)
by John M. Ott, taken from his book, _Health and Light_

While in process of trying to decide how much ultraviolet to give the animals, my wife and I had dinner one evening in a restaurant known as "**Well of the Sea," in the basement of the Hotel Sherman in Chicago.** As soon as we entered the restaurant I noticed that there were black light ultraviolet lights placed throughout the ceiling. They had been installed solely for ornamental purposes, to cause designs on the waiters' coats, as well as the menus, to fluoresce in the otherwise subdued light. The next morning I went back to the restaurant with a meter to measure the intensity of the ultraviolet at various distances from the ceiling, such as table level and the eye level of the waiters as they walked directly under the various light fixtures.

\==================================================

[https://chuckmanchicagonostalgia.wo...nly-hotel-with-drive-in-garage-drawing-c1960/](https://chuckmanchicagonostalgia.wordpress.com/2010/03/08/sherman-hotel-well-of-the-sea-restaurant-only-hotel-with-drive-in-garage-drawing-c1960/postcard-chicago-sherman-hotel-well-of-the-sea-restaurant-only-hotel-with-drive-in-garage-drawing-c1960/)

Jack Kruse said:
There is another trick I use on T2D when I am going to operate on them and they have a **high HbA1c: I have them use d-glucosamine** for two weeks pre op and them put them on the Leptin Rx with a lot of seafood. d-Glucosamine (GlcN) is a freely available and commonly used dietary supplement potentially promoting cartilage health in humans, which also acts as an inhibitor of glycolysis.
\-----------------------------------------------
Jack Kruse said:
I have always been interested in glucosamine's use with cancer patients and in those wishing to extend lifespan: The mechanism by which D-Glucosamine inhibits glycolysis is by inhibiting hexokinase (or the liver analog, glucokinase). This impairs glucose metabolism and results in the cell “switching” over to amino acid metabolism to produce energy. Thus D-Glucosamine is a “metabolic switch” that converts “Warburg-type metabolism” in the cell (i.e. aerobic glycolysis in the presence of adequate oxygen) over to “protein metabolism”. 100 microM of Glc-N results in a 43% inhibition of glucose oxidation. I know of no studies however that shows whether we can get this effect from oral doses of the supplement.
\-----------------------------------------------

+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Synthesis of pre-vitamin D3 in the skin involves UVB radiation which
effectively penetrates only the epidermal layers of skin.
7-Dehydrocholesterol absorbs UV light most effectively at wavelengths
between 290-320nm and thus the production of vitamin D3 will only occur
at those wavelengths. The two most important factors that govern the
generation of pre-vitamin D3 are the quantity (intensity) and quality
(appropriate wavelength) of the UVB irradiation reaching the
7-dehydrocholesterol in the middle of the skin layers in the stratum basale
and stratum spinosum.

### JanSz — 2015-09-17T17:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-177282>

Light affects humans and animals.
Light (and other near visible light) frequencies,
(their densities and exposure time) affect:

cortisol (level and profile)
melatonin (level and profile)
vit D (and vit D,sulfate)
melanin

\-----------------------------------------------------------
Products on this web site
[http://www.amjoshop.com/category-s/45.htm](http://www.amjoshop.com/category-s/45.htm)

are already divided into numerous categories.

Specifically they make distinction between UV-A and UV-B sources.
(So there is assumption that the lights marked as
UV-A have minimal UV-B content and vice versa.

There is distinction made along energy produced by the source.
Low level, meant to be used indefinitely.
High level, meant for therapeutic use, minutes.

I don't have time to sit in front of the sun during 6 days a way for a long period of time. That would be an amazing luxury. I have a few minutes tops. I have four kids that go to school and I work early Saturday (before the sun gets up). Sunday is my day.

JanSz: Would love if you could help with his. I have not been able to find the answer in any video you sent, chart, or graph. I don't see where Jack has ever answered this question.

Assuming I have a 2 - 5 minutes to look at the sun during the week before 10 am:

What's the minimum time one should look at the sun in minutes?

As I've stated before, I have a hard time looking directly at the sun, even when it first rises. So I try to look directly at it with my eyes closed.
Is it okay to keep your eyes closed when looking directly in the sun or does that defeat the purpose?

If I'm not supposed to look directly at the sun, using a clock for an example, and going clockwise with the sun being 12, where should I be looking? 3 o'clock, 4? 5? 9? Or all?

Sundays - it's my day and can do whatever I want. I can sit for hours in front of the sun till 10 am. I would just like some direction on how best to visually look while being in the presence of the sun.

Thank you.

Frank

@[Frank Kaplan](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17930/)

There is at least two reasons why we need exposure to light.

To maintain Circadian Rhythm (I think less time is required but it must happen at the correct time or close to it)
Now we are concious about benefits of (UV-A)+(UV-B). They are blocked by eyelids. So the eyes must be open.
Newer look directly at the sun.
Look under the sun.
Think of eyes as camera. You do not want to have a picture that includes sun.

Remember light is not only about eyes, it is also about whole body skin.
Remember skin must be conditioned to accept sunlight.
You newer want to get sunburn.

\======================

\======================
When one is commited to indoor living most of the time:
Based on my understanding of what @[Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/) teaches I propose this:

When sunrise to sundown happens outdoor (we want indoor)
Black Light
Reptile UVB (replicating desert)
High intensity white light (replicating equator)(aquarium lights)
Overall I think this may end up being good reef aquarium lights (actinic + white 10-20K)

Sundown to bed time
Black Light
Red Light

Bed time
no lights (or just tiny red)

/// (IR still thinking about) either as IR bulbs or sauna
(sauna + CT spa in the (sun) room lit with black lights)
(automatically movable glass in the sun room)
(I could use that) (hint hint).

\-------
Sources of black light and reptile UVB

### JanSz — 2015-09-18T19:24:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-177337>

> [Frank Kaplan said:](/goto/post?id=177330)
>
> Good stuff. Thank you. You said: "Remember skin must be conditioned to accept sunlight." What does this mean?

After prolonged lack of sun exposure one must start slowly.

There is a video that describes introduction to the whole body sun exposure.
It describes it in (rather drastic) way.
It takes about a month (of incremental daily 5min exposures)
to be able to stay in full sun (for half an hour)(safely).
It takes longer if one want to stay whole day in full sun.
No sunscreens. Nothing applied to skin.
No glasses.
No clothes.

.....
Divide body into 7 parts
1-feet to mid way feet knees.
2-feet to knees
3-feet to mid thighs
4-feet to crotch
5-feet to bottom of rib cage
6-feet to neck
7-whole body
\---------------------
Start with lying down, only part #1 exposed to sun for 5 minutes
rest of the body covered
next day
expose part #2 for 5 minutes
and so on
so when you come first time to the whole body you will have
5 min on face and 35 min on part #1

Then keep increasing by 5 min every day.

\=================
With effort as above,
it is better to go thru it just once,
and keep more frequent sun exposure.

/

What you do about it? Diet? Anything else?

My (incomplete) answer is:
do (good) Fatty Acids analysis.
I do this one at LabCorp (it is actually done at Mayo Clinic.
It is part of LONG LIST for LabCorp:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/)

specifically:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

I post-process results, as shown on the bottom of post #20 of pg-e2-ratio thread.

\===================================
There is lots of talk about
how important DHA is
how good it is to eat fish and seafood
Omega6/Omega3 ratio 4:1 being good.

All that is important, for sure.
But
it is good to remember this:
\-------
[https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](https://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)
The other interesting factor I have seen few speak of, is the requirement that each organ of the body has for omega 6 and 3 fats. I decided to look into this issue more several years ago when I was researching my own health. I always believed that the brain had a large omega 3 component naturally and it turned out, I was dead wrong. The brain makes up 3% of our body weight (BW) but has a 100 to 1 ratio of _**06 to 03 within it normally**_.
\----
\-----------------
Omega6/Omega3 ratio 4:1 being good:
good for what?
it is good to have that ratio in the diet we eat (says Yehuda and dr Patricia Kane follows that recomendations)
Different organs have different ratios.
Good blood test, (the one I posted above), having optimal results for each component of Omega6 and Omega3,
have ratios:
Total O6/Total O3 = 10.9
AA/DHA = 7.178
LA/ALA = 34
DHA/EPA = 2.45
AA/EPA = 17.6
\========================
But dr Patricia Kane uses best test and that other test (Krieger) comes with much different ratios:

Total O6/Total O3 = 4.8
AA/DHA = 3.65
LA/ALA = 92.9
DHA/EPA = 12.4
AA/EPA = 45.4

To get those values I assume each component value is equal to average of their range value.
Same approach as dr Kane uses when evaluating results (page#2 post #27 PG/E2 ratio thread). That is I assume %Status for each fatty acid category=zero. Each one is in mid-range.

//

The same thing hold true when you are holding your cell phone or laptop. You become dehydrated like a comet or like California is now. Think about physics.......it is tied to life too. [http://www.cbsnews.com/news/water-blasts-on-comets-tied-to-sunlight-cycle/](http://www.cbsnews.com/news/water-blasts-on-comets-tied-to-sunlight-cycle/)

### JanSz — 2015-09-24T22:41:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-177812>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cooking-with-blood.16117/page-2#post-177802](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/cooking-with-blood.16117/page-2#post-177802)

\[ quote="Jack Kruse, post: 170692, member: 1031"\]Ok...........enough with the nonsense. Lets get back to science. This thread is filled with nuggets.

Why you better understand UV light better than you currently do. The photoelectric effect basis is buried in its unusual ability and maybe why life chose it to signal.

The dualism to the nature of light is best demonstrated by the photoelectric effect, where a **"weak" UV light produces a current flow (by releases electrons to its surrounding) but a strong red light does not release electrons no matter how intense the red light is.** Red light then acts a type of cellular "capacitor or battery" for energy. This is very similar to water in a sense in a cell. Where is the energy from red light stored you ask? In cell membranes, water, and in mitochondria of all cells.

Einstein explained that light exists in a particle-like state as packets of energy (quanta) called photons. **The photoelectric effect occurs because the packets of energy carried by each individual red photons are too weak to knock the electrons off the atoms no matter how many red photons you beamed onto the cathode (metal). But the individual UV photons were each strong enough to release the electron and cause a current flow.** It appears that life needs a chronic stream of UV light to work properly. UV light is also at low energy parts of the electromagnetic spectrum because life's job is to move electrons to and fro to alter the redox chemistry of proteins with those electrons. Since light and the periodic table are quantized.........this means that life also likely seeks out atoms on the early part of the periodic table to build its proteins. That also turns out to be factual.\[/quote\]

### JanSz — 2015-09-28T22:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-178058>

> [SpiritGuide said:](/goto/post?id=178017)
>
> I just found this article, it's very interesting...
>
> I hope we begin to see these types of advancements implemented into all electronic devices and tech gear.
>
> [http://tek-think.com/2015/08/27/physicists-discover-a-way-to-confine-electromagnetic-radiation/](http://tek-think.com/2015/08/27/physicists-discover-a-way-to-confine-electromagnetic-radiation/)

Physicists Discover a Way to Confine Electromagnetic Radiation
27 Aug 2015
\------------
Physicists have found a radical new way confine electromagnetic energy without it leaking away, akin to throwing a pebble into a pond with no splash. The theory could have broad ranging applications from explaining dark matter to combating energy losses in future technologies.

However, it appears to contradict a fundamental tenet of electrodynamics, that accelerated charges create electromagnetic radiation, said lead researcher Dr Andrey Miroshnichenko from The Australian National University (ANU).

“This problem has puzzled many people. It took us a year to get this concept clear in our heads,” said Dr Miroshnichenko, from the ANU Research School of Physics and Engineering.

The fundamental new theory could be used in quantum computers, lead to new laser technology and may even hold the key to understanding how matter itself hangs together.

Dr Miroshnichenko commented:

Ever since the beginning of quantum mechanics people have been looking for a configuration which could explain the stability of atoms and **why orbiting electrons do not radiate.**

The absence of radiation is the result of the current being divided between two different components, a conventional electric dipole and a toroidal dipole (associated with poloidal current configuration), which produce identical fields at a distance.

If these two configurations are out of phase then the radiation will be cancelled out, even though the electromagnetic fields are non-zero in the area close to the currents.

Dr Miroshnichenko, in collaboration with colleagues from Germany and Singapore, successfully tested his new theory with a single silicon nanodiscs between 160 and 310 nanometres in diameter and 50 nanometres high, which he was able to make effectively invisible by cancelling the disc’s scattering of visible light.

This type of excitation is known as an **anapole** (from the Greek, ‘**without poles**’).

Dr Miroshnichenko’s insight came while trying to reconcile differences between two different mathematical descriptions of radiation; one based on Cartesian multipoles and the other on vector spherical harmonics used in a Mie basis set.

“The two gave different answers, and they shouldn’t. Eventually we realised the Cartesian description was missing the toroidal components,” Dr Miroshnichenko said.

We realized that these toroidal components were not just a correction, they could be a very significant factor.

Dr Miroshnichenko said the confined energy of anapoles could be important in the development of tiny lasers on the surface of materials, called spasers, and also in the creation of efficient X-ray lasers by high-order harmonic generation.
\=========================================================

[https://answers.yahoo.com/question/...HBvcwMxBHZ0aWQDBHNlYwNzYw--?qid=1006012408543](https://answers.yahoo.com/question/index;_ylt=AwrTccdTlwlWcF4A7O8nnIlQ;_ylu=X3oDMTByNWU4cGh1BGNvbG8DZ3ExBHBvcwMxBHZ0aWQDBHNlYwNzYw--?qid=1006012408543)

**Why electrons in a particular orbit of an atom do not radiate energy?**
The earlier theory says that any charge will radiate energy if it cahnges direction or magnitude or both.

Best Answer:
Start thinking of electrons in an atom as probability clouds...or regions of space around the atom at a given radius where the probability is high that the electron will exist. Bohr theory ("orbits") is not complete, in fact it's not an accurate depiction of the electrons in an atom.

The reason the electron's don't radiate while "orbiting" is that they don't "orbit" anything!! Instead, there are regions of space around the atom where the probability of an electron existing at any instant is non-zero. Excited electrons may lose energy and drop to a lower energy level (to a smaller radius from the atom), in which case the electron will radiate energy in the form of a photon whose energy is equal to the difference in energy levels (excited state - lower state).

As a concrete example, this radiation from dropping to lower energy states is the basis of lasing action and the principle on which lasers operate.

As I said, think in terms of the quantum mechanical description of the electron rather than the Bohr theory. It's not over your head, believe me, just study quantum mechanics. Start with the electron in a Hydrogen atom, it's the simplest case (a 1 electron atom!!)

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal-halide\_lamp#Color\_temperature](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal-halide_lamp#Color_temperature)
**Output spectrum of a typical metal-halide lamp**

...............................................................

Ultraviolet A UVA **320 – 400nm**
Long wave, **black light**, not absorbed by the ozone layer
\--------------------------
Ultraviolet B UVB **290 – 320nm**
Medium wave, mostly absorbed by the ozone layer
\--------------------------
Ultraviolet C UVC 100 – 290nm
Short wave, germicidal, completely absorbed by the ozone layer and atmosphere
**\------------------------------------------------------------
I note that spectrum of typical metal halide lamp ends at 350nm.**

**So there is only part of UVA and no UVB.**

**If above applies to all metal halide lamps,
that would explain why actinic fluorescence tubes are often/always added to salt water aquariums. Often prepacked in one enclosure Metal Halide + **actinic fluorescence tubes.**

-   [Hydrargyrum medium-arc iodide lamp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hydrargyrum_medium-arc_iodide_lamp), high power MH lamps as used in cinematography

\===========================================================
**Mercury-vapor lamp**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercury-vapor\_lamp#Emission\_line\_spectrum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercury-vapor_lamp#Emission_line_spectrum)

**Emission line spectrum**
The strongest peaks of the emission line spectrum are[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercury-vapor_lamp#cite_note-8)[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mercury-vapor_lamp#cite_note-9)

[](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:HG-Spektrum_crop.jpg)

Line spectrum of mercury vapor. The blue-green tint of mercury vapor lamps is caused by the strong violet and green lines.
Wavelength (nm) Name (see [photoresist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photoresist)) Color
184.45 ultraviolet (UVC)
253.7 ultraviolet (UVC)
365.4 I-line ultraviolet (UVA)
404.7 H-line violet
435.8 G-line blue
546.1 green
578.2 yellow-orange
In low-pressure mercury-vapor lamps only the lines at 184 nm and 253 nm are present. Only the light at 253 nm is usable. Synthetic quartz can be used in the manufacturing to keep the 184 nm light from being absorbed. In medium-pressure mercury-vapor lamps, the lines from 200–600 nm are present. The lamps can be constructed to emit primarily in the UV-A (around 400 nm) or UV-C (around 250 nm). High-pressure mercury-vapor lamps are those lamp commonly used for general lighting purposes. They emit primarily in the blue and green.
