### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-11T04:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-179034>

Color me confused that you are confused. Carroll is a IR expert........not a solar expert and not even close to a UV expert. He understands one part of light. Light is never designed to work in isolation, it works in a symphony with other parts of the environment working with light not against it. Blue light makes ROS in the singlet state. In fact, I dont think he understands much about UV light at all. Not sure what youre concerned about here. Why aren't you or Krista concerned about how people live in artificial lit environments for 16 hours a day for their entire life? Isn't that environment temperature controlled with respect to the outdoors? What about the electromagnetic fields inside that house and how it effects the quantum yield of the fake light in their eyes or on their skin? Isn't living indoors as a human akin to placing a tarp over a cactus tree in the desert? Why no concern about this? Why would adding UVA light indoors to non full spectrum light not be equivalent? Might adding UV to fake light improve the situation if they left the fluorescents or LED's off? Is UVA exposure on the left side of your face through a truck window the same as using an indoor UVA bulb? Do you know what frequencies are blocked by the glass and what frequencies are given by the UV light bulb? Do we have any stories of humans around UVA indoor light for over 20 years to make a comment on this? Check. We do. Well of the Sea restaurant in Chicago. Indoor UV light is not the same as solar UV light through automotive glass. People will do bio hacks and learn the result for themselves and go from there. I am a bit shocked you are worried about UVA but not about blue 420-465. That has been constantly on since 1924 and the evolution of blue light innovation has far outpaced human evolution. While this is going on, we have demonized UV light and tried to bury it.

We have rods and cones. We have melanopsin. All plug into the RPE to regenerate. Rods are used in dark. Melanopsin is used in changing light environments when light is going from bright to dark. Rods and melanopsin regenerate in the daytime............when we dont have to use them as we are designed. What regenerates them? The RPE does. It is brown and it has dense cores in its hexagon cells that spin faster as UV light hits it to turn short wavelength light to a large DC electric current and makes ocular melatonin. Ocular melatonin can store more UV light. Your chaired has melanin and carotenoids that also store UV light. Why? Might we use that store to regenerate Cones at night? That current regenerates rods and melanopsin during DAYLIGHT hours when UV light is present. If you are modern zoo animal called a human do you ever face full solar spectrum to regenerate rods or melanopsin these days? NOPE. So what might your bio hack entail given these variables?

Cones are used for the eye camera. We use them in daytime and they regenerate at night. What does that? Stored UV light and Red light in tissues. Red light penetrates deeply and UV light only penetrates 1/10 of a mm so it needs a storage depot to do things at night. How we assimilate stored UV light is tied to ROS levels and their state. Blue light at night makes boatloads of ROS in the singlet state. You think that is good for UV assimilation and future use? Might that be a problem by itself? How might one bio hack that very issue?

The atmosphere filters UV light from the sun. ozone takes out most UVC and most UVB. The atmosphere has a stable ratio of nitrogen to oxygen in air. it is 3.727 on every square inch of this planet regardless of location. In this way nitrogen and oxygen are glued together mechanically to act as a layer. What do you know about light at layers or interfaces? Might this be a way to keep the quantum yield of solar light in a tight range? nitrogen and oxygen sounds a lot like RNS and ROS in tissues does n't it? Did you know isolated blue light from fake sources makes a shit load of ROS?

Did you know that solar light releases massive amounts of RNS from skin stores?

Did you know that does not happen when you are in fake blue light?

What does this? You need UV and IR light to get RNS from the skin. What happens when it is missing? high blood pressure........CVA, MI, heart disease.........etc.
Do zoo animals have this mix of light naturally in their cages they call house, cars, schools, jobs, etc........? why aren't you worried about this more?
Do members here know a bit about red light now? Yep.
What don't they know a lot about yet? Purple light. Are they hearing a lot about that now? Who has demonized purple light and why?
How might they learn about purple light? A bio hack maybe?

UV light is assimilated differently in environments as temperature and solar condition vary with respect to light. Fake UV light and full spectrum light paint very different biologic pictures. Since the WHO has made UV light a class 1 carcinogen, on this basis they should also make oxygen a class 1 carcinogen. Photochemical damage, known as sun burn, is not a UV light story alone. You have to have both UV light OVER EXPOSURE and oxygen present to get one. Have you ever heard of anyone getting a sunburn from a light bulb that makes UVA light alone?

You have to have both UV light over exposure and excessive ROS species for a sunburn to occur. Why is that we blame UV light for the skin damage and not oxygen in this case? You do know that indoor fake light makes ROS in the singlet state. Have I mentioned that ROS in that state causes sun and wind burn? Oxygen free radicals studied alone without their proper environmental context have been shown to be the first step in oncogenesis in many organs. Are you all worried about that hack? Should you both stop breathing? Try living without oxygen for some time. Its not pleasant. I know I have done that hack. Read Tensegrity 5. The same crazy idea is now being perpetrated on Earth with respect to UV light; What might happen if we live an entire life without purple UV light? OK.........the main point. UV and IR have to be matched. What do you know about IR light and night. Yep......it is present then too if we allow it to be. So UVA light at night is not a problem if IRA or NIR are present too. Infrared is stored in us and our environment called the ground if we were in the sun during the day or we slept on the ground. We don't. Do most of us collect IR during the day in our tissues. My people do..............or at least they should.

The irony is that oxygen and UV light are coupled environmentally in our atmosphere by ozone and full spectrum UV light. People forget these fundamentals, but I don’t. If we are too take the WHO seriously, and bury the sun as they suggest, then maybe we should all stop breathing oxygen too, huh? Should we do a RCT on this idea? Modern evidence based science is ludicrous when you really see how questions are being studied. Sunlight provides a full spectrum and all experiments need to study it as such. Short wavelengths can stress a cell but the longer IR A wavelengths are designed to raise ATP to improve wound healing. IR A light is always present when UVA and/or UVB are present in a day or season in any location on Earth. We seem to forget this basic fact. You cannot subtract any part of the spectrum and draw a conclusion about the biologic effect of purple light alone. Their blinders to these basic facts should show you why artificial indoor light is so deadly. It gives us a spectrum of light that is akin to alien sun.

In equatorial areas, temperatures are homogenous and UVB an UVA with IR are fairly consistent. This means time exposure is the key to assimilation in these areas. Skin color is also a huge factor as is the persons age. Outside tropic of cancer and capricorn temperature is quite variable and so is UV light. That means it is a hackable event at night if your tissues have IRA and NIR stored in them. Still think clothes are good. See why I say CT is always a win now? That means assimilation is not time dependent but dependent upon the quality during seasons and the environment's quantum yield for light.

Indoor lighting is filled with short wavelength blue light 420-465nm that gives a stress response to the autonomic nervous system at the PVN. This increases vibrations across all membranes. That degrades signaling optically. In this case, a smart clinician should realize this paradox of modern living and make the suggestion to some that they consider using black lights indoors or use vitamin D pills to maintain their D3 levels to summer time levels since they live in a homogenous temperature year round..................

### JanSz — 2015-10-11T18:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-179090>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infrared](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infrared)

Commonly used sub-division scheme

Division Name Abbreviation Wavelength Frequency Photon Energy
Near-infrared **[NIR, IR-A DIN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DIN)** [0.75–1.4 µm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%9Cm) [214-400 THz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/THz) [886-1653 meV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeV)
Short-wavelength infrared SWIR, IR-B DIN [1.4-3 µm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%9Cm) [100-214 THz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/THz) [413-886 meV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeV)
Mid-wavelength infrared [MWIR, IR-C DIN; MidIR.\[12\] Also called intermediate infrared (IIR)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infrared#cite_note-rdmag20120908-12) [3–8 µm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%9Cm) [37-100 THz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/THz) [155–413 meV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeV)
Long-wavelength infrared LWIR, IR-C DIN [8–15 µm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%9Cm) [20-37 THz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/THz) [83–155 meV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeV)
Far-infrared FIR [15–1,000 µm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/%CE%9Cm) [0.3-20 THz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/THz) [1.2–83 meV](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MeV)

NIR and SWIR is sometimes called "reflected infrared," whereas MWIR and LWIR is sometimes referred to as "thermal infrared."
Due to the nature of the blackbody radiation curves, typical 'hot' objects, such as exhaust pipes,
often appear brighter in the MW compared to the same object viewed in the LW.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

CIE division scheme
The International Commission on Illumination (CIE) recommended the division of infrared radiation into the following three bands:\[13\]
**IR-A** 700 nm – 1400 nm (0.7 µm – 1.4 µm, 215 THz – 430 THz)
IR-B 1400 nm – 3000 nm (1.4 µm – 3 µm, 100 THz – 215 THz)
IR-C 3000 nm – 1 mm (3 µm – 1000 µm, 300 GHz – 100 THz)
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ISO 20473 scheme

Designation Abbreviation Wavelength
**Near-Infrared NIR** 0.78–3 µm
Mid-Infrared MIR 3–50 µm
Far-Infrared FIR 50–1000 µm
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>
> i am therefore having trouble seeing people sleeping under UVA globes all night (black light) despite most modern humans missing this end of the spectrum all day, is it not a mismatch at night?
> can someone please help me with this, is it a power story whereby they are far enouggh from the lamp that it is not having an effect?
>
> i have just done a course with James Carroll and he was quite concerned after hearing the story of the hacks, but i guess i am missing something...he didnt know a lot about UV...couldnt answer questions with any gusto on this end of the spectrum other than saying it is "ionising"
>
> i am looking for a straight answer not a nugget at this stage if possible to help get over the confusion...
>
> josh

Josh,
I was glad that you have brought up the safety issues. I think all of us have to make the effort to look at safety as a subject within any subject on this blog involving bio hacks. These posts and blogs are read by every sort of person from teenagers to elderly people and they may simply follow ideas and recommendations without realizing the safety issues that may apply to them.

Comparative Examples: CT can crash a persons health or promote health or kill. I know about this because I had a very close call even though I was following the recommendations found on this site. Eventually Jack issued some warning that adrenal function must be up to the task but still says "the more CT the better" which obviously, is a false statement.

Jack insisted his epi paleo diet is primordial, (implied that it will fit everyone) then along came Jeremy and the diet made him sick and would have lead to an early demise had he persisted too long.

Ketosis was promoted by Jack for years without any cautions that I can remember. Now Jack speaks of people going into coma if their systems Mitochondria are not healthy...

Now we have the incredible information coming from Jack about light being the smallest and yet the most powerful of Quantum bio hacking tools. "Most powerful" should be a red flag warning that if it's that powerful then along with it's life supporting functions it could be life harming if used improperly. For me this is basic thinking. Too much Good light becomes Bad light if it's not done right. So parsing out the use UVA UVB IR becomes very important.

I believe that your insistence in getting the safety issues clarified is what each and every reader of this blog must do as thoroughly as possible. And because you treat others as a physician it's a thousand times more important for you to know that subject to the greatest extent possible.

I think Jack truly loves the beauty of the science of causation and effect that he has uncovered and shared but I also think his lack of cautions on this site is a serious issue.
His almost daily expressions of anger are hard to understand but he did say in the epi paleo book that he is "in a rage" regarding the present state of medicine. I personally think that rage might be the most seldom needed emotion of all. People don't need rage to think, (hard to think clearly when raging...) or improve things, or to help people..
Thanks,
Brother John

Infrared is stored in us and our environment called the ground if we were in the sun during the day or we slept on the ground. We don't. Do most of us collect IR during the day in our tissues. My people do..............or at least they should.
>
> The irony is that oxygen and UV light are coupled environmentally in our atmosphere by ozone and full spectrum UV light. People forget these fundamentals, but I don’t. If we are too take the WHO seriously, and bury the sun as they suggest, then maybe we should all stop breathing oxygen too, huh? Should we do a RCT on this idea? Modern evidence based science is ludicrous when you really see how questions are being studied. Sunlight provides a full spectrum and all experiments need to study it as such. Short wavelengths can stress a cell but the longer IR A wavelengths are designed to raise ATP to improve wound healing. IR A light is always present when UVA and/or UVB are present in a day or season in any location on Earth. We seem to forget this basic fact. You cannot subtract any part of the spectrum and draw a conclusion about the biologic effect of purple light alone. Their blinders to these basic facts should show you why artificial indoor light is so deadly. It gives us a spectrum of light that is akin to alien sun.
>
> In equatorial areas, temperatures are homogenous and UVB an UVA with IR are fairly consistent. This means time exposure is the key to assimilation in these areas. Skin color is also a huge factor as is the persons age. Outside tropic of cancer and capricorn temperature is quite variable and so is UV light. That means it is a hackable event at night if your tissues have IRA and NIR stored in them. Still think clothes are good. See why I say CT is always a win now? That means assimilation is not time dependent but dependent upon the quality during seasons and the environment's quantum yield for light.

Jack,
A long time ago you made a post entitled something like: "How can I improve?" a lot o folks wrote in with suggestions for the website. I did not see that as answers for your question as you could easily have said "How can the website be improved?" but that was not your question. So I'm going to make a suggestion on how You can improve, (for your stated mission of improving medicine and helping people live optimally).

I think you should look at your teaching method or model. Are you trying to emulate a pissed off Moses when he finds his subjects worshiping the wrong thing? Are you emulating a fire and brim stone Old Testament preacher, breathing fire on the congregation? Or are you copying that teaching professor from your school days that could fillet the skin off of a student's back with just a look or a few choice words?

Often you come across as caring, giving, sharing, strong, amazingly insightful and determined and other times a very very different message is conveyed. I won't put words to that, I don't see the point of doing so, I think in your heart of hearts you know what I mean.

My second suggestion is this: Know your intention for every word and sentence you write. Not only what is the science but what is the overall intention for the reader.....
I like and appreciate the additional info that you provided Josh and us all but the anger behind it was so off base...
Thanks,
Brother John

> then after sundown you are adding blue and green that should not be there, that confuses circadian signaling even more.

> ...............................................................
>
> Ultraviolet A UVA **320 – 400nm**
> Long wave, **black light**, not absorbed by the ozone layer

> Medium wave, mostly absorbed by the ozone layer

> My wife was using (actinic light = black light) in the end of the assumed tropical day, in her salt water aquarium. (That may have been either mistake or accounting for possible difference in light content between equatorial region and sun light we have in northern hemisphere, north of Northen Tropic, Tropic of Cancer.
>
> \-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
> The central retinal-SCN-hypothalamic-pituitary axis needs 290-415 nm light exposure in the AM to function properly. This is why your hormones from the anterior pituitary are almost exclusively released between 6-10 AM as I laid out in the Cold Thermogenesis 7 blog post years ago. This is also why I been telling my members **as soon as they rise they need to go look in the direction of the rising sun _and basking their skin_ in it for period of time as soon as they rise. I also warned them not do it behind glass, plastic, or glasses, ever.** Now you know why I said it.
> \-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
> Then I thought about Feymann and what he did, and why he did it. I realize immediately, this long standing meme exists publicly and in medicine too. I realized this meme was false physically, yet it is still present today. Full spectrum sunlight, natural sunlight contain 290-400 nm light. All life evolved with this light on earth. How could it be bad? I then found out almost all manufactured lenses of any kind back from 1930 until today are made so they block all UV frequencies of light. The reason for this fear is the result of a general lack of understanding in the differences of the relative intensities of the near or long wavelengths UV light and how our atmosphere allows 290 and above in regular sunlight every morning. The confusion exists because there is far and short wavelength UV that appears in natural sunlight at the surface of Earth. **Day light has shorter wavelength UV in 290-380 range, where twilight has the 381-500 range blue light.** I found out that these frequencies of light interact with different proteins in our eye to do different things. The atmosphere filters and stops virtually all of the far short wave (UVA), except for trace amounts. Natural sunlight, however, allows quite a bit of near long wavelength UV light.
> \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Spectral Distribution of Energy From the Sun
> [http://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/jres/53/jresv53n2p113\_A1b.pdf](http://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/jres/53/jresv53n2p113_A1b.pdf)
> \------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

>

>
>
>
> ..

Human origins are in Great Rift Valley.
Say Lake Victoria. Practically equator.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Full light zone. 12hr day/ 12hr night
No clothes, no eye glasses, no fire, no artificial lights
During day when we would feel too much light, shade under tree was sometimes available.

Now we live everywhere on the globe, varying light conditions,
**Some of us want to recreate light conditions that are closer to our original design, which is tropical or equatorial light**

\=================================
From pov of natural sunligh exposure earth can be divided into number or areas.
Mostly by latitude but also ozone hole over Australia is separate.
Ozone hole allows for extra dose of UVB.

TROPICS
either of the two parallels of terrestrial latitude at a distance of about 23-1⁄2 degrees north or south of the equator.

Subtropics
The subtropics are geographic zones located roughly between the tropic circle of latitude (the Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn) and the 38th parallel in each hemisphere.

Temperate zone
extends from 38th parallel to 66degree 33minutes in each hemisphere

\===================================
Solar spectrum

Some Sunlight that reaches ground level is absorbed, assume that it is done uniformly except for ozone hole.
Sunlight that reaches upper atmosphere have vavelengths in range 250-2500nm.

Ultraviolet C or (UVC) range, which spans a range of 100 to 280 nm.
Ultraviolet B or (UVB) range spans 280 to 315 nm
Ultraviolet A or (UVA) spans 315 to 400 nm
Visible range of light spans 380 to 780 nm
Infrared range that spans 700 nm to 1,000,000 nm (1 mm)
Infrared-A: 700 nm to 1,400 nm
Infrared-B: 1,400 nm to 3,000 nm
Infrared-C: 3,000 nm to 1 mm
A typical human eye will respond to wavelengths from about 390 to 700 nm

violet 380–450 nm
blue 450–495 nm
green 495–570 nm
yellow 570–590 nm
orange 590–620 nm
**red 620–750 nm**

\===================================

**Light can be stored by body for latter use.

During day time naturally we benefit from UVB to IR-A
They are present at full density only in tropics.
At night time in tropics (and elsevere) only IR-A available.**

**Visible light at night is harmfull to us, except for red light.
UV-A or UV-B are normally absent at night but we benefit from their supplementation (except in ozone hole)**
To benefit from UVB & UVA we need red and IR-A to be present.
Subtropical areas allows for sufficient amount of red and IR-A (stored).
**Supplementing in other than tropical or subtropical areas (at night) requires red light**.
But this may possibly be discussed further since crew at Well of the Sea restaurant in Chicago had only UVA & UVB).

When supplementing with light during day time, it can be done as during night time,
but preferably Full Spectrum Reptile lights could be used.
The advantage of Full Spectrum Reptile lights is that they can properly lit the room,
also their light output is known and make user realize that numerous light bulbs are required,
and even with that it is hard to come close to desirable light densities.

\---------------------------------------------------
Example
Assume that person is located 36" from light bulb.
Solar Glo 160W
UVB output 25 μW/cm^2
The seller of that light says:
On strong sunny day in middle of USA one may get 150 μW/cm^2 UVB
**That would require 6 of this bulbs in one place to replicate mid USA light density.**
Mid USA is located in temperate zone.
Geographic center of the contiguous United States
LAT. 39°50' LONG. -98°35'
\----------------------------------------------------
The latitude and longitude of Forked River, New Jersey is:
39° 50' 23" N / 74° 11' 25" W
Oyster Creek Nuclear Power Plant
Latitude: 39° 48.85' Longitude: -74.2063889
I live 90 miles north of this latitude.
\----------------------------------------------------

.

> Some Sunlight that reaches ground level is absorbed, assume that it is done uniformly except for ozone hole.
> Sunlight that reaches upper atmosphere have vavelengths in range 250-2500nm.
>
> Ultraviolet C or (UVC) range, which spans a range of 100 to 280 nm.
> Ultraviolet B or (UVB) range spans 280 to 315 nm
> Ultraviolet A or (UVA) spans 315 to 400 nm
> Visible range of light spans 380 to 780 nm
> Infrared range that spans 700 nm to 1,000,000 nm (1 mm)
> Infrared-A: 700 nm to 1,400 nm
> Infrared-B: 1,400 nm to 3,000 nm
> Infrared-C: 3,000 nm to 1 mm
> A typical human eye will respond to wavelengths from about 390 to 700 nm
>
> violet 380–450 nm
> blue 450–495 nm
> green 495–570 nm
> yellow 570–590 nm
> orange 590–620 nm
> **red 620–750 nm**
>
>
>
>
>
> \===================================
>
> **Light can be stored by body for latter use.
>
> During day time naturally we benefit from UVB to IR-A
> They are present at full density only in tropics.
> At night time in tropics (and elsevere) only IR-A available.**
>
> **Visible light at night is harmfull to us, except for red light.
> UV-A or UV-B are normally absent at night but we benefit from their supplementation (except in ozone hole)**
> To benefit from UVB & UVA we need red and IR-A to be present.
> Subtropical areas allows for sufficient amount of red and IR-A (stored).
> **Supplementing in other than tropical or subtropical areas (at night) requires red light**.
> But this may possibly be discussed further since crew at Well of the Sea restaurant in Chicago had only UVA & UVB).
>
> When supplementing with light during day time, it can be done as during night time,
> but preferably Full Spectrum Reptile lights could be used.
> The advantage of Full Spectrum Reptile lights is that they can properly lit the room,
> also their light output is known and make user realize that numerous light bulbs are required,
> and even with that it is hard to come close to desirable light densities.
>
> \---------------------------------------------------
> Example
> Assume that person is located 36" from light bulb.
> Solar Glo 160W
> UVB output 25 μW/cm^2
> The seller of that light says:
> On strong sunny day in middle of USA one may get 150 μW/cm^2 UVB
> **That would require 6 of this bulbs in one place to replicate mid USA light density.**
> Mid USA is located in temperate zone.
> Geographic center of the contiguous United States
> LAT. 39°50' LONG. -98°35'
> \----------------------------------------------------
> The latitude and longitude of Forked River, New Jersey is:
> 39° 50' 23" N / 74° 11' 25" W
> Oyster Creek Nuclear Power Plant
> Latitude: 39° 48.85' Longitude: -74.2063889
> I live 90 miles north of this latitude.
> \----------------------------------------------------
>
>
> .

Specifications

Mega-Ray® SB 275-watt narrow-flood is a 275-watt Self Ballasted Par-38 True Frosted Flood Lamp. A true flood lamp, it will disperse usable UVB in a 30" circumference at a 24" distance.

### JanSz — 2015-10-15T00:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-179493>

[https://www.jackkruse.com/physician-guest-blog-2-the-invisible-sun-and-purple-light/](https://www.jackkruse.com/physician-guest-blog-2-the-invisible-sun-and-purple-light/)
\======================================================
“You don’t need to speak Chinese to like Chinese food.” – it isn’t necessary to understand every detail of:

electron spin,
the retinohypothalamic tract and
RPE,
melanopsin,
methylation,
biological semi-conduction,
quantum tunneling,
SIRT1,
NAD+/NADPH ratios,
triplet oxygen and the
EZ water, etc.
to be able to apply this information.
\--------------------------------------------------------------
**Electron spin**
Electron magnetic moment
In atomic physics, the electron magnetic moment, or more specifically the electron magnetic dipole moment, is the magnetic moment of an electron caused by its intrinsic properties of spin and electric charge.

\--------------------------------------------------------------
**Retinohypothalamic tract**
The retinohypothalamic tract (RHT) is a photic neural input pathway involved in the circadian rhythms of mammals.\[1\] The origin of the retinohypothalamic tract is the intrinsically photosensitive retinal ganglion cells (ipRGC), which contain the photopigment melanopsin. The axons of the ipRGCs belonging to the retinohypothalamic tract project directly, monosynaptically, to the suprachiasmatic nuclei (SCN) via the optic nerve and the optic chiasm.\[2\]\[3\] The suprachiasmatic nuclei receive and interpret information on environmental light, dark and day length, important in the entrainment of the "body clock". They can coordinate peripheral "clocks" and direct the pineal gland to secrete the hormone melatonin.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The retinohypothalamic tract transmits information on light levels from the eyes to the hypothalamus

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**RPE,
Retinal pigment epithelium**
The pigmented layer of retina or retinal pigment epithelium (RPE) is the pigmented cell layer just outside the neurosensory retina that nourishes retinal visual cells, and is firmly attached to the underlying choroid and overlying retinal visual cells.

Function
The RPE has several functions,\[5\] namely,
light absorption,
epithelial transport,
spatial ion buffering,
visual cycle,
phagocytosis,
secretion and
immune modulation.

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**melanopsin,**
Melanopsin is a type of photopigment belonging to a larger family of light-sensitive retinal proteins called opsins and encoded by the gene Opn4.\[1\] Two other opsins in the mammalian retina are both involved in the formation of visual images: rhodopsin and photopsin in the rod and cone photoreceptor cells, respectively.

Melanopsin is found in intrinsically photosensitive retinal ganglion cells (iPRGCs) in mammalian vertebrates. In non-mammalian vertebrates, however, melanopsin is found in the retina as well as in other tissues, including but not limited to the brain, the pineal gland, and the skin. iPRGCs are photoreceptor cells which are particularly sensitive to the absorption of short-wavelength visible light and communicate information directly to the area of the brain called the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), otherwise known as the central "body clock" in mammals.\[2\] Consequently, melanopsin plays an important non-image-forming role in the setting of circadian rhythms as well as other functions. Mutations in the Opn4 gene can lead to clinical disorders, such as Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD).\[3\]

Function
Melanopsin-containing ganglion cells, like rods and cones, exhibit both light and dark adaptation; they adjust their sensitivity according to the recent history of light exposure.\[14\] However, while rods and cones are responsible for the analysis of images, patterns, motion, and color, melanopsin-containing ipRGCs contribute to various reflexive responses of the brain and body to the presence of light.\[7\]

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**Methylation,**
In the chemical sciences, methylation denotes the addition of a methyl group to a substrate or the substitution of an atom or group by a methyl group. Methylation is a form of alkylation with a methyl group, rather than a larger carbon chain, replacing a hydrogen atom. These terms are commonly used in chemistry, biochemistry, soil science, and the biological sciences.

In biological systems, methylation is catalyzed by enzymes; such methylation can be involved in modification of heavy metals, regulation of gene expression, regulation of protein function, and RNA processing. Methylation of heavy metals can also occur outside of biological systems. Chemical methylation of tissue samples is also one method for reducing certain histological staining artifacts. The counterpart of methylation is demethylation.

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**biological semi-conduction,**
[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?search=biological semi-conduction&title=Special:Search&fulltext=1](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?search=biological+semi-conduction&title=Special%3ASearch&fulltext=1)
The page "Biological semi-conduction" does not exist. You can ask for it to be created, but consider checking the search results below to see whether the topic is already covered.

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**Quantum tunnelling**
Quantum tunnelling or tunneling (see spelling differences) refers to the quantum mechanical phenomenon where a particle tunnels through a barrier that it classically could not surmount. This plays an essential role in several physical phenomena, such as the nuclear fusion that occurs in main sequence stars like the Sun.\[1\] It has important applications to modern devices such as the tunnel diode,\[2\] quantum computing, and the scanning tunnelling microscope. The effect was predicted in the early 20th century and its acceptance as a general physical phenomenon came mid-century.\[3\]

Tunnelling is often explained using the Heisenberg uncertainty principle and the wave–particle duality of matter. Pure quantum mechanical concepts are central to the phenomenon, so quantum tunnelling is one of the novel implications of quantum mechanics.

\----------------------------------------------------------------
**Sirtuin 1**
Sirtuin 1, also known as NAD-dependent deacetylase sirtuin-1, is a protein that in humans is encoded by the SIRT1 gene.\[1\]\[2\]\[3\]

SIRT1 stands for sirtuin (silent mating type information regulation 2 homolog) 1 (S. cerevisiae), referring to the fact that its sirtuin homolog (biological equivalent across species) in yeast (S. cerevisiae) is Sir2. SIRT1 is an enzyme that deacetylates proteins that contribute to cellular regulation (reaction to stressors, longevity).\[4\]

Function
Sirtuin 1 is downregulated in cells that have high insulin resistance and inducing its expression increases insulin sensitivity, suggesting the molecule is associated with improving insulin sensitivity.\[5\] Furthermore, SIRT1 was shown to de-acetylate and affect the activity of both members of the PGC1-alpha/ERR-alpha complex, which are essential metabolic regulatory transcription factors.\[6\]\[7\]\[8\]\[9\]\[10\]\[11\]

\-----------------------------------------------------------------
**NAD+/NADPH ratios,**

Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD)
Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (NAD) is a coenzyme found in all living cells. The compound is a dinucleotide, because it consists of two nucleotides joined through their phosphate groups. One nucleotide contains an adenine base and the other nicotinamide. Nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide exists in two forms, an oxidized and reduced form abbreviated as NAD+ and NADH respectively.

In metabolism, nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide is involved in redox reactions, carrying electrons from one reaction to another. The coenzyme is, therefore, found in two forms in cells: NAD+ is an oxidizing agent – it accepts electrons from other molecules and becomes reduced. This reaction forms NADH, which can then be used as a reducing agent to donate electrons. These electron transfer reactions are the main function of NAD. However, it is also used in other cellular processes, the most notable one being a substrate of enzymes that add or remove chemical groups from proteins, in posttranslational modifications. Because of the importance of these functions, the enzymes involved in NAD metabolism are targets for drug discovery.

Concentration and state in cells
The balance between the oxidized and reduced forms of nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide is called the NAD+/NADH ratio. This ratio is an important component of what is called the redox state of a cell, a measurement that reflects both the metabolic activities and the health of cells.\[16\] The effects of the NAD+/NADH ratio are complex, controlling the activity of several key enzymes, including glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase and pyruvate dehydrogenase. In healthy mammalian tissues, estimates of the ratio between free NAD+ and NADH in the cytoplasm typically lie around 700; the ratio is thus favourable for oxidative reactions.\[17\]\[18\] The ratio of total NAD+/NADH is much lower, with estimates ranging from 3–10 in mammals.\[19\] In contrast, the NADP+/NADPH ratio is normally about 0.005, so NADPH is the dominant form of this coenzyme.\[20\] These different ratios are key to the different metabolic roles of NADH and NADPH.

\-----------------------------------------------------------------
**triplet oxygen** and the

This article has multiple issues.
Triplet oxygen, systematically but less commonly, 1,2-dioxidanediyl, is a term that refers to normal, gaseous oxygen (O2, dioxygen) in its ground state. It is therefore classified as an inorganic chemical, and more specifically as a particular electronic state of one allotrope of the inorganic chemical element, oxygen. In this particular state, according to one type of modern bonding theory, the electron configuration of the oxygen molecule has two electrons occupying two molecular orbitals (MOs) of equal energy (that is, degenerate MOs), therefore remaining unpaired. These orbitals are classified as antibonding and are of higher energy, so the resulting bonding structure between the oxygen atoms is weakened (i.e., is higher in energy)—for instance, it is higher in energy than the bonding in dinitrogen, where bonding MOs are filled instead. The spectroscopic molecular term symbol for triplet (ground state) oxygen is 3Σg−.\[2\]\[3\]
\------------------------------------------------------------------

**Spin**
The s=1/2 spins of the two electrons in degenerate orbitals gives rise to 2x2=4 independent spin states in total. Exchange interaction splits these into a singlet state (total spin S=0) and a set of 3 degenerate triplet states (S=1). In agreement with Hund's rules, the triplet states are energetically more favorable, and the ground state of the molecule carries an electron spin of S=1. Excitation to the S=0 state results in much more reactive, metastable singlet oxygen.

Because the molecule in its ground state has a non-zero spin magnetic moment, oxygen is paramagnetic; i.e., it can be attracted to the poles of a magnet. The Lewis structure O=O does not accurately represent the diradical nature of molecular oxygen; molecular orbital theory must be used to adequately account for the unpaired electrons. Triplet oxygen is better described by a bond order of one and two halves instead of two to better reflect its unpaired bonding electrons. This allows for easier reasoning of the bond length.

\-----------------------------------------------------------------
**EZ water**, etc.
The page "EZ water" does not exist. You can ask for it to be created,
[https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?search=EZ water&title=Special:Search&fulltext=1](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?search=EZ+water&title=Special%3ASearch&fulltext=1)

The Fourth Phase of Water - What You Don’t Know About Water, and Really Should
[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/08/18/exclusion-zone-water.aspx#](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/08/18/exclusion-zone-water.aspx#)!

The Fourth Phase of Water: Beyond Solid, Liquid, and Vapor,

inherent differences between regular water and EZ water include its structure. Typical tap water is H2O but this fourth phase is not H2O; it’s actually H3O2. It’s also more viscous, more ordered, and more alkaline than regular water, and its optical properties are different. The refractive index of EZ water is about 10 percent higher than ordinary water. Its density is also about 10 percent higher, and it has a negative charge (negative electrical potential). This may provide the answer as to why human cells are negatively charged.

What Creates or Builds EZ Water?

One of the greatest surprises is that the key ingredient to create EZ water is light, i.e. electromagnetic energy, whether in the form of visible light, ultraviolet (UV) wavelengths and infrared wavelengths, which we’re surrounded by all the time. Infrared is the most powerful, particularly at wavelengths of approximately three micrometers, which is all around you. The EZ water can build on any hydrophilic or water-loving surface when infrared energy is available.

It builds by adding layer upon layer of EZ water, and can build millions of molecular layers. This is how it occurs in nature. For example, ice doesn’t form directly from ordinary H2O. It goes from regular water to EZ water to ice. And when you melt it, it goes from ice to EZ water to regular water. So EZ water is an intermediate state.

“Glacial melt is a perfect way to get EZ water. And a lot of people have known that this water is really good for your health,” Dr. Pollack says.

Testing water samples using a UV-visible spectrometer, which measures light absorption at different wavelengths, Dr. Pollack has discovered that in the UV region of 270 nanometers, just shy of the visible range, the EZ actually absorbs light. The more of the 270 nanometer light the water absorbs, the more EZ water the sample contains. EZ water appears to be quite stable. This means it can hold the structure, even if you leave it sitting around for some time. Water samples from the river Ganges and from the Lourdes in France have been measured, showing spikes in the 270 nanometer region, suggesting these “holy waters” contain high amounts of EZ water. According to Dr. Pollack, there’s compelling evidence that EZ water is indeed lifesaving...

\-----------------------------------------------------------------

Many apologies for the repost! I would like to seek advice regarding UVA lights as I am only able to find blacklight blue tubes in Singapore. The one that I have is an 8W from sankyo-denki and I am using it at my office desk. I am wondering what wattage should I get (i.e. is 8W enough?) and if there is any safety distance that I should keep it away from me (would it have x-ray emissions? it is about 35 inches away from me). Many thanks!

Information extracted from [http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2\_06.html](http://www.sankyo-denki.co.jp/e2_06.html)

These lamps effciently emit near ultraviolet rays at 315nm- 400nm which have strong photochemical and fluorescent effects.
They are special deep blue filter glass which absorbs visible rays and transmits near ultraviolet rays and two types of phosphor are used with radiation peak at 352nm and 368nm, respectively.
They are used for tests, inspections, examinations, and investigations in various fields, as well as for stage illumination in theaters and nightclubs, advertisements and sign boards.

on this thread, page 18 there are this two lines giving information on natural UVB density reaching earth.
I assume that one does not want to go over 250 uW/cm2
\------
**On strong sunny day in middle of USA one may get 150 μW/cm^2 UVB

50-250 uW/cm2 mimics the natural environment**
\---------------------------------------------------------
For simplicity, it is easier to think of lamp similar to Solar Glo 160W (or similar build)
Assume that your proposed lamp is a point,
sitting within round reflector of 45 degree angle
shining at the wall,
all power lits area that have diameter of 2 meters and area π\*2^2/4=3.14 m^2

To get at the wall average power density 1 μW/cm^2 UVB, point must emit
1μW/cm^2 \* 3.14 m^2=3.14 W

1 μW =1 microwatt = 0.001 milliwatt
1 μW = 0.001 mW
1μW=0.000001 W

(0.000001 W/cm^2) \* 3.14 \* (1000000 cm^2)=3.14 W

**To get 50 μW/cm^2 UVB at 1 meter distance from lamp, lamp must emit 3.14\*50=~150 watts UVB
To get 150 μW/cm^2 UVB at 1 meter distance from lamp, lamp must emit 3.14\*150=~450 watts UVB
To get 250 μW/cm^2 UVB at 1 meter distance from lamp, lamp must emit 3.14\*250=~750 watts UVB**

Your biggest lamp that you propose uses 40W and emits 8.1W of light 320-400nm
so it is UVA light only

\=====================================================

Ultraviolet C or (UVC) range, which spans a range of 100 to 280 nm.
Ultraviolet B or (UVB) range spans 280 to 315 nm
Ultraviolet A or (UVA) spans 315 to 400 nm
Visible range of light spans 380 to 780 nm
\=====================

Note that one reptile lamps I posted on this thread, Reptile UVB200 emits 225

@[Curves](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1418/)
As I am continuing in this light research, I progressively bought
Reptile UVB150
Reptile UVB200
Solar Glo 160
Each of these lights have a frequency charts.
Those charts may be misleading because vertical axis shows Relative Spectra Pover (%).
So the Reptile lights use substantial % of their power to produce UVB and UVA,
and Solar Glo uses very small % of their power to produce UVB and UVA.
But all that is relative.
There are tables on each of those bulbs. They show power density of UVB light.
That table is what I am using.
Reptile UVB150 gives one, 1 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb
Reptile UVB200 gives six, 6 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb
Solar Glo 160 gives one 25 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb

Those lamps are meant for terrariums.
The Solar Glo is meant to provide UV, light and heat in one piece of equipement.

////
See also post 377. Dr Kruse says that those tables are inaccurate
(or possibly I have not used then in right way).
Anyway, it looks like the amount of vit D produced may be overly optimistic.
Unless something changes after next webinar I think I will get another solar Glo.

### Curves — 2015-10-19T18:17:13+0300

> Hi Curves,

Hi @[JMO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/403/)! I use the exact clip on lamp that @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) posted above. Great minds think alike. They are super cheap and come in a few sizes. Just make sure the size you buy can take a 160W bulb like @ncid and JanSz mentioned above.

What's good about the clip on is that you have flexibility with the height and can alter it easily so it hits the parts of your body you want exposed easily. The negative is that you need something to clip it on. I clipped it to a traditional light stand so I can add other bulbs like more UV if I choose.

Despite the heavy cloud cover outside, I'm naked at my desk in front of the Solar Glo and it feels great.

### nicld — 2015-10-26T19:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180500>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=178081)
>
> UVA1 phototherapy
> [http://www.uptodate.com/contents/uva1-phototherapy](http://www.uptodate.com/contents/uva1-phototherapy)
> Ultraviolet A1 (UVA1) is a newer form of phototherapy that uses only the longer,
> non-erythemogenic UV wavelengths (340 to 400 nm),
> thus reducing the risk of sunburn reactions associated with the shorter-wavelength
> UVA2 (320 to 340 nm) and UVB (290 to 320 nm).
> \==================
> UVB therapy (broadband and narrowband)
> [http://www.uptodate.com/contents/uvb-therapy-broadband-and-narrowband?source=see\_link](http://www.uptodate.com/contents/uvb-therapy-broadband-and-narrowband?source=see_link)
> Broadband ultraviolet B (UVB) radiation (280 to 320 nm),
> 311 to 313 nm (narrowband UVB)
> \===========================
>
> Psoralen plus ultraviolet A (PUVA) photochemotherapy
> [http://www.uptodate.com/contents/psoralen-plus-ultraviolet-a-puva-photochemotherapy?source=see\_link](http://www.uptodate.com/contents/psoralen-plus-ultraviolet-a-puva-photochemotherapy?source=see_link)
> Psoralen plus ultraviolet A (PUVA) photochemotherapy combines the administration of psoralens, a class of phototoxic plant-derived compounds, with an exposure to ultraviolet A radiation (UVA)
>
>
>
>
> .

### JMO — 2015-10-28T23:38:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180657>

I've been using my black Duracell light in a small lamp next to my laptop (orange acrylic over screen) along with a reptile UVB 150 while working the entire day for a couple weeks now. This has been an incredible change in my energy, my brain fog lifting, and my mood. I really haven't done anything that has measured up to this (hope it stays). I have been getting outside for a bit in the am too. This summer I was out a lot in the am sun even on conference calls for longer periods but never got the affect I'm getting from these lights indoors. Just wondering if this is telling me something about where I live. I'm in Omaha NE which is not a huge city but is something blocking UVA in my environment? I would think all the time I've spend outside in the summer would have been a bigger bang. Outside should be better than inside. Finally charging my battery (all the DHA I've been collecting eating cases of oysters over the past year).

One bad effect I had was with my gut. I usually don't have any issues but may have overdid the light last Thurs when I went in my Mercola tanning stand bed for 10 min each side. I worked with the lights all day (which should be okay because Well of the Sea employees did and people work in fake/wrong spectrum light everyday) and also used my UFO light on my lower back for 15 min. I was filled with energy and really good things but had the worst diarrhea ever but didnt feel sick at all. Finally getting better after 4 days. Just connecting this to the cruise where I had slight gut issues that I normally don't have---thinking more UVA/B in caribbean. Earlier in the week, randomly switched from coffee to green tea. Also, eating more Bubbies sauerkraut and dark Choc. Maybe all this energy purged some bad bacteria or maybe I ate a bad oyster or my SCN clock and gut is mismatched. I don't think my gut has been working for a long time due to all the years working in blue light on a laptop. Hope all the light in the right spectrum will help.

### nicld — 2015-10-28T23:55:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180658>

That is great.

For me, my UFO light makes me go when done and so does raw sauerkraut. Having dealt with constipation for so many years I welcome free movements

Oh there was an article that I just read that talked a little about doing the LED treatment around the navel area (lower back should be the same) but I cannot find it now. I think I got it fromt he drop box on the quantum age. If I find it, I will let you know.

### JanSz — 2015-10-29T01:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180663>

> [JMO said:](/goto/post?id=180657)
>
> I've been using my **black Duracell light** in a small lamp next to my laptop (orange acrylic over screen) along with a **reptile UVB 150** while working the entire day for a couple weeks now. This has been an incredible change in my energy, my brain fog lifting, and my mood.

Every little bit counts,
but Duracell light is likely less potent than UVB150
And UVB150 have lowest potency of the three:

UVB150
UVB200
Solar Glo 160W

Reptile UVB150 gives one, 1 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb
Reptile UVB200 gives six, 6 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb
Solar Glo 160 gives one 25 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb

Anyhow looks like we are on the right track. (Thank you Jack !!!!)

FWIW, it is good to know that we are responding to those lights.

Warren is about 26" from one Solar Glo 160
and reported some changes.

I am glad for all this info, because I am 3 feet from two Solar Glow 160 and one UVB200
and
do not feel much difference.

> And UVB150 have lowest potency of the three:

> UVB150
> UVB200
> Solar Glo 160W
>

> Reptile UVB200 gives six, 6 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb
> Solar Glo 160 gives one 25 μW/cm^2 of UVB at 36" from bulb

the conclusion that therefore using UVB200 or UVB150 is more profitable is off.

to produce same amount of UVB power
if those bulbs were at 36" from you

### JanSz — 2015-10-31T17:39:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180840>

Please review this video describing how dminder works.

As it is now, **I think that it DOES NOT measure actual exposure to UV-B.
It estimates it based on computer input and data tables.
My next door neighbor have lots of clouds, I have cloudless sunny day.
We have same UV index published by Weather channel. (time 2:56/4:10)**

It warns about skin burning.
Skin burning is a recent factor when people are not seeing enough sun and their skin is not conditioned. People with skin long term accustomed to sun (in their area) can stay outside whole day, naked. At worst they may go into shade under tree, still naked.
(Alexander Wunsch MD video)(below), there is description on how to condition skin to sun exposure. It takes whole month of daily incremental exposures to get there.

I was expecting that input is coming via cell phone camera.
I was expecting ability to check what my Solar Glo lamp produces (regardless of time of the day or latitude and altitude.

It takes all kind of factors into consideration (good)
lattitude, altitude
time of the day
age
skin type
% exposed skin
but
for UV-B exposure takes published UV index

Also, it is Politically Correct from pov of UV-A.
It warns about skin damage due to UV-A.
People using it may tend to stay inside more, missing on grounding and sun exposure, afraid of sun.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

Hey JanSz - I recently purchased the setup you were posting ( ^^this lamp with the 160w UV-A/UV-B powersun bulb).

### JanSz — 2015-11-01T04:35:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-180874>

> [twilightkid said:](/goto/post?id=180865)
>
> Hey JanSz - I recently purchased the setup you were posting ( ^^this lamp with the 160w UV-A/UV-B powersun bulb).
> I'm having some trouble coming up with a plan for mounting the lamp, since I noticed the directions say the bulb must be horizontal or it voids the warranty. Nonetheless, Would you mind sharing your personal usage and or the amount of time you expose yourself to the bulb in a sitting?

My lamps are above me and are at 45degree angle from horizontal (or vertical).
I have three lamps in the cluster, two Solar Glo 160 and one UVB200.
They are 36" from my shoulders.
I spend lots of time under them.
I do not count time.
Originally from Poland.
I am white Caucasian with originally very white skin, but have a deep brown suntan.
If you have white skin and have not been exposed much to sun, think about how this lamp affects you.
If it does, keep some limit on exposure for 2-3 weeks.
After that you should be good for unlimited exposure (I guess).

/
Something is not right about horizontal position of lamp.
This lamp are meant for terrariums.
Dedicated housings for this lamps are sold to mount them in vertical position over terrarium.

### JanSz — 2015-11-04T16:34:49+0200

>
> I'm just not sure that buying random black lights and exposing oneself to them all day and maybe at night is a very good idea.

Blue light is visible light.
One should be able to confirm its existence using simple spectrometer as we were discussing earlier on this thread,
but your test with blue blockers already gives good suspicion.

Anyhow, using blue light at night (during sleeping time) is a sort of desperate attempt at increasing fertility.
When one is trying to conceive, this may be good (temporary) workaround.

Otherwise, for the sleeping time (say 8hrs) red, IR-a, should be ok,
forget UV-A & UV-B unless we can find lamps that are tested, and do not have any blue.
There are Woods Lights, but they are UV-A only.

UV-A---> thyroid
UV-B---> vit D

During daytime, cloudy or sunny, we should maximize time outdoor.
When indoor, Solar Glo 160W provides UV-A, UV-B, visible light and IR-A.
It is designed at tested on reptiles.
\------

\------
For me, the still open question is about using eye protection:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-22#post-180986](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/page-22#post-180986)

### JanSz — 2015-11-04T19:55:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-181156>

[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse)
November 4 at 12:40pm
Today's group PSA about eyes: Contacts filter out purple light and this atrophies the eye muscles of the globe elongate. This makes vision less sharp because blue light enters and it bends most through the anterior eye where the prisming of light occurs. Blue light falls anterior to the macula to cause visual blur. The more visual blur with a lack of purple light one gets the more chance dry macular degeneration occurs because we need purple light to hit the retina and specifically the macula to stimulate blood flow to the surface of the retina just as we do in the skin.

Dan Oren, MD, PhD, researcher for Yale University and the National Institutes of Health (NIH) indicated that 50 percent of the entire blood volume in the body passes through the eyes in 40 minutes, and that there is a biochemical mechanism of light involving **hemoglobin within the blood (280 nm light)** , allowing the eyes to be an appropriate portal for phototherapy treatment. Nature uses this effect to maintain vision. That is all. Carry on.

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1090819547597605/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1090819547597605/)

\------------------------------
[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi)If you understand this............what does this do in a blue lit world longer term. How many of you beside [Tom Doud](https://www.facebook.com/tom.doud?hc_location=ufi) know about the prisming effect of blue light that causes visual blur? And what this does to the eye clock mechanism longer term............I'm dropping nuggets now.
\------------------------------
[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi)Jack is just warming up for the new series............and it is a whopper.

I use two Solar Glo 160 W.
They were together at 36" distance from my right shoulder.
Few days ago changed arrangement. I have one on left the other on right side, both still at 36" distance.
I also have UVB200 on right side, I think I will get rid of it.

For over a week I was sleeping with one black light.
It bothers me, and I think I know why. Not proven, just a guess.
There is still lots of blue (visible light) there since I can see it.

My day = 14 hrs= 8AM -10PM (when I am indoor, Solar Glow 8AM till 9PM or longer, then I use red
My night = 10 hrs, 8hrs sleep (total darkness), 2 hrs in between (red)

Solar Glow have IR-A as heat

Note, house heating system is IR-A light.

......
On post #443
note the color chart from flux.
Go into the link.
Note that lots of blue is suppressed after sundown, by flux.

\------
The bottom line is shape of cortisol curve using blood or better saliva test.

The ultimate is saliva
cortisol, DHEA, melatonin.

I tested my blood 10/20/15
cortisol(7;30AM)=17.4ug/dL(6.2-19.4)
cortisol(1PM)=8.6ug/dL(2.3-11.9)
I think it is not bad for 76yo.

### JanSz — 2015-11-07T17:25:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-181331>

> [Curves said:](/goto/post?id=181304)
>
> You are a rock star @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
>
> So you keep the S9lar Glo on until 9:00 pm. or longer? As if you lived at the equator? I didn't think of that! It's not a mismatch with your local time? I'd be happy frankly to have more light at night.

Day at equator is terrible (in my book).
Vacation there are obviously ok, but not for permanent living.
I lived for one complete year at about southern tropic, Brazil, Angara Dos Reis.
Days there are about 12hr, nights there are 12hr long, every day, 365 then repeat.
Days are bright sunny hopeful, and I felt that they will last forever, well, at least till 9PM.
EVERY DAY I was surprised, by night that arrived at 6PM.
I felt cheated.

I was raised in Poland, my known ancestors are from there.
Longest day, June 21, 16hr 25 min

Since 1972 I am in NJ 07054
Longest day, June 21, 15hr 5 min

São Paulo,
Longest day, Dec 21, 13hr 35min

Bambari, Central African Republic
Could not find anything close to Lake Victoria, but close enough.
5° 45′ 55″ N, 20° 40′ 27″ E
12hr/12hr day/night
\----------------------------------------
Since now I live a lot in a self created zoo cage,
I think that 14 hr day and 10 hr night should be a good compromise for year round living.
\-----------------------

[http://www.timeanddate.com/sun/brazil/sao-paulo](http://www.timeanddate.com/sun/brazil/sao-paulo)
.

I TOTALLY understand this. I grew up near where you are now just outside of Manhattan in Westchester County (40.9) When I left home, I moved into the City. God, I was crazy back then but those are stories not to be told LOL. When I was ready to leave Manhattan I moved to San Francisco (37.7833° N) which I never would have left if it wasn't for Dr. Kruse. For the last two years I'm in Oregon (45.6142° N). When I lived in San Francisco the length of the days and nights felt "right." I was comfortable with the extremes during the summer and winter solstice. What I find here in Oregon, although I love where we live in the shadow of a rain forest surrounded on three sides by big giant Douglas Fir trees and far enough from neighbors and cell towers and I'm only a few degrees north of my birth latitude, I friggin hate the extremes, especially the long days near the summer solstice. There's more to it, but I haven't quite put my finger on it yet. There is something I don't yet understand about the seemingly foreign way the sun travels in the sky. Still trying to figure it out.

I also find that I can't leave the Solar Glo in one spot. I literally have to move it from left to center to right throughout the day like it's orbiting my head to feel good.

So, I will experiment with extending my day with my Solar Glo and see how that feels. These somehow already puts me at ease.

P.S. My paternal grandfather was Polish too  He was awesome.

### JanSz — 2015-11-09T16:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-181475>

I think different colors are better at different times of day, even more than what f.lux allows currently, but I'm probably biased ^\_^
@lorna
[https://justgetflux.com/forum/topic/322/in-an-office-with-no-windows-therefore-no-sunlight](https://justgetflux.com/forum/topic/322/in-an-office-with-no-windows-therefore-no-sunlight)
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------

different colors are better at different times of day

Above feels right.

But living in zoo in a (comfortable) cage, what to do with that idea?

Intensity also differ throughout the day.
That is easier,
say
near sunrise and sunset ----> one Solar Glo 160W
10AM-2PM --------------------> three Solar Glo 160W
the rest of the day ------------> two Solar Glo 160W

Draw back of above is that the colors are constant.

\------------------------------
Well, lets see some of the differences.
Courtesy of flux
[https://fluxometer.com/rainbow/#!id=Outdoors/4PM LA shade&age=76](https://fluxometer.com/rainbow/#!id=Outdoors/4PM%20LA%20shade&age=76)

LA morning 10AM
vs
LA afternoon 4PM

Charts look similar, but note the intensity difference (vertical scale)
152 μW/cm^2 --- 10AM
10.1 μW/cm^2 --- 4PM(shade)
\-------------------------------------------------

Overall, the differences are mostly in intensity (and easier to manipulate, 1,2 or 3 lights).

### cinnamon — 2015-11-13T01:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-181838>

Regarding supplementing with oral D3, read [Tensegrity #7: Black Hole Sun, Here We Come](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-7-black-hole-sun-come/)

Here is an excerpt:

Today, those low in Vitamin D3 think taking an oral supplement is as good as being in the sun. Dermatologist also believe this. This is also not true, in my opinion, if you read the old research on Vitamin D. Bicknell and Prescott found in 1953 in their exhaustive research that that “sunlight acting directly on the body should be the way in which Vitamin D is obtained”. **They found in their research that the gut and body have have no power to regulate the amount of vitamin D it absorbs from food**. Foods rarely have Vitamin D3, so from an evolutionary perspective this is not a big issue. This is likely why there is no gut feedback. Today, most humans low in Vitamin D, are now relying on their gut to supplement Vitamin D because they are not getting any sun. That implies we might want to be careful about what we are doing today to treat low Vitamin D levels with an oral supplement.

This means taking vitamin D orally has no true negative feedback control normally. Oral vitamin D will still help increase your plasma levels but this may not be what you want to do until you understand why you vitamin D level is low to begin with in today’s world.
​

The whole of this blog is important stuff!

### JanSz — 2015-11-17T21:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-182209>

[http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse](http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse)

November 17, 2015
BP5 • Dr Jack Kruse • Quantum Sleep and Living a Optimized Life

[http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse](http://www.beastified.com/bp1/2015/9/19/bp3-dr-jack-kruse)

**EPISODE BREAKDOWN:**

-   What is sleep? And why is it important?
-   Is there a maximum hours length of sleep needed or a minimum number of hours needed?
-   Can sleep help prevent degenerative aging diseases and cancer?
-   Correlation between melatonin and gaining a night of good healthy sleep?
-   Does blue light at night cause melatonin production to cease?
-   The effects of blue light on the pineal gland?
-   How does sleep work to regenerate us?
-   Is sleep the primordial condition or do think it evolved as we did?
-   Sun Exposure in relation to just jumping straight in shower after?
-   Correlation between Sex and good quality sleep?
-   What are Dreams Are Made Of? Do they hold any significant value to our health?

### JanSz — 2015-11-18T16:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-182263>

**Natural Sleep and Its Seasonal Variations in Three Pre-industrial Societies**
[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1096383350374558/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1096383350374558/)

[http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822(15)01157-4](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/abstract/S0960-9822%2815%2901157-4)

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574)

**Highlights**
•Preindustrial societies in Tanzania, Namibia, and Bolivia show similar sleep parameters
•They do not sleep more than “modern” humans, with average durations of 5.7–7.1 hr
•They go to sleep several hours after sunset and typically awaken before sunrise
•Temperature appears to be a major regulator of human sleep duration and timing

**Summary**
How did humans sleep before the modern era? Because the tools to measure sleep under natural conditions were developed long after the invention of the electric devices suspected of delaying and reducing sleep, we investigated sleep in three preindustrial societies \[[1](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib1), [2](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib2) and [3](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib3)\]. We find that all three show similar sleep organization, suggesting that they express core human sleep patterns, most likely characteristic of pre-modern era _Homo sapiens_.

Sleep periods, the times from onset to offset, averaged 6.9–8.5 hr, with sleep durations of 5.7–7.1 hr, amounts near the low end of those industrial societies \[ [4](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib4), [5](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib5), [6](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib6) and [7](http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0960982215011574#bib7)\].

There was a difference of nearly 1 hr between summer and winter sleep.

Daily variation in sleep duration was strongly linked to time of onset, rather than offset.

None of these groups began sleep near sunset, onset occurring, on average, 3.3 hr after sunset. Awakening was usually before sunrise.

The sleep period consistently occurred during the nighttime period of falling environmental temperature, was not interrupted by extended periods of waking, and terminated, with vasoconstriction, near the nadir of daily ambient temperature.

The daily cycle of temperature change, largely eliminated from modern sleep environments, may be a potent natural regulator of sleep.

**Light exposure was maximal in the morning and greatly decreased at noon,** indicating that all three groups seek shade at midday and that light activation of the suprachiasmatic nucleus is maximal in the morning.

Napping occurred on <7% of days in winter and <22% of days in summer.

Mimicking aspects of the natural environment might be effective in treating certain modern sleep disorders.

..............
Tanzania/Coordinates
**6.3070° S**, 34.8540° E
Arusha - 1400m/4594ft
.................
Namibia/Coordinates
**22.5700° S**, 17.0861° E
The mean altitude of Windhoek is 1,657 metres (5,436 ft)
.................
Bolivia/Coordinates
**16.7120° S**, 64.6660° W
La Paz altitude at over 3500m above sea level,
.................

Lake Victoria
1.0000° S, 33.0000° E
The lake's surface is _3,720 feet_ (_1,134 metres_) above sea level, and its greatest ascertained depth is 270 feet (82 metres).

.

My sleep is relatively light and I wake up couple times during night.
The black light during night time (experiment) was a fiasco for me. Felt better after I stopped using it.
When I get off bed I am newer groggy.
On blood tests my cortisol 7;30AM, noon, 2;30PM look rather well.

I drink coffee daily, usually start 1/2 hr after getting off bed, in small sips, one mug lasts me till 1PM, usually I drink 1/2 mug the rest of the day.
1mug of my coffee contains heaping teaspoon of instant Maxwell House.

Blood drawn for tests when I am about 9hr + fasted, drank 16 oz water 2hrs earlier.

What I noticed though, is that coffee although I don't need it to feel awake or energized or alert, it does help me get super focused and hyper productive and I love that feeling. If I drink it too early in the morning prior to eating it just makes me feel anxious so I wait until mid-morning.

### JanSz — 2015-11-19T17:03:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-182343>

[https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1097089826970577/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/872967099382852/1097089826970577/)

Janno Joona
16 mins

Today I had the chance to interview one of the most cited scientists in the world from my neck of the woods - **Tiina Karu.** She is a highly respected **expert on photobiomodulation and red light.** Strangely, nobody knows about her in Estonia, since she works in Russia. A lot of deep science and interesting stories...

Couple of interesting things she mentioned:

1\. **Exaggerating with red light can be harmful (she and her colleagues lost their sleep after working with red light for long periods).** It's quite hard to do, but still possible. Irritability, restlessness and loss of sleep, in her opinion, are signs of overdosing. So that should be tracked when biohacking.

2\. Results are diffent, depending on the person. And their current situation (redox). **The worse the initial condition, the bigger the (healing) effect.**

3\. Blue light is not always harmful. It can be healing in smaller doses. But it's way easier to hurt yourself with that. Blue light won't penetrate as deep as red light, but intensity is much higher.

4\. Even if working in a pitch black lab, indoors, with the same red wavelength - she recieved **different results depending on the season**. Talk about quantum queerness...

5\. She has used red light for over 30 years to treat her health problems. Mainly an old sport injury.

6\. Oh yeah. When I tried to brag about my CTing and swimming in an ice cold lake in the morning, she said she did the same. But in the Sea.

\------------

Elly Thekla Philipp the seasonal differences are interesting----wonder what she was testing it with, plant or animal or ?
Like · Reply · 12 mins
Janno Joona
Janno Joona Yeah, she suspected some kind of cellular memory. But not sure at all.
Otherwise she pretty much **agreed everything Rub Sal** and others have been saying here. And she said to start slowly and to be careful when biohacking.
Like · Reply · 1 · 8 mins
Veronika Blazkova
Veronika Blazkova thanks for sharing, Janno. I feel it pairs the best with full solar AM spectrum in long light cycle season/zone. Just my experience of course.
Like · Reply · 1 min
Janno Joona
Janno Joona Can't remember which exact cells she irradiated, but I think she saw the difference in results pretty much evey time. Results were more clear in winter. She also mentioned that people in North have better results than the ones closer to the equator.

Gretchen Bronson Love it. **And Rub has mentioned that the Red UFO 660nm after dark can have negative affects on sleep and other things.** Just htis morning I had the urge to pull out the Red 660nm UFO light and I attribute that to the cold, grey and rainy day... felt super charged this am.. b/c of this..

1- dose
2- single frequency (LED)

concentrated dose of (single frequency) red light over sore joint is different than one small (CFL) red lamp to light the room at night (during wakeup time).

CFL - compact fluorescent light
LED - Light Emitting Diode

LED - high concentration light
CFL - lower concentration light (relatively to LED)

\======================
Circadian clocks are endogenous timers adjusting behaviour and physiology with the solar day
1\. Synchronized circadian clocks improve fitness
2 and are crucial for our physical and mental well-being
3\. Visual and non-visual photoreceptors are responsible for synchronizing circadian clocks to light
4,
5, but clock-resetting is also achieved by alternating day and night temperatures with only 2–4 °C difference
6,
7,
8\. This temperature sensitivity is remarkable considering that the circadian clock period (~24 h) is largely independent of surrounding ambient temperatures1, 8.
\---------------------------------------------
deltaC 2-->3.6F
deltaC 4-->7.2
(2–4) °C=~(4-7)F

### JanSz — 2015-11-28T17:26:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-182771>

[https://www.facebook.com/yewwei.tan?fref=ufi](https://www.facebook.com/yewwei.tan?fref=ufi)
(2) UV light is a stressor if appropriate biological transducers or capturing agents are not present. Primary compounds are Melanin (which absorbs UV-A) and 7-dehydrocholesterol (which absorbs UV-B to form Vitamin D).

(3) Sunlight contains a big mix of frequencies. The red light and IR frequencies are likely good even with high levels of exposure because of the effect they have on cell water, mitochondria, and cell signalling. Frequencies of light more energetic than red usually require pigments for capture (again, melanin can absorb all of this, step down the energies, and then re-emit IR light)

Nitric Oxide still bad in excess.

UV light still bad in excess.

### JanSz — 2015-11-28T18:35:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-182778>

[http://tanyewwei.com/blog/ct-hormetic/](http://tanyewwei.com/blog/ct-hormetic/)
A successful response to cold exposure involves either maintaining core body temperature, or even raising it slightly. This is a good indicator of a successful response to Cold Thermogenesis, and one possible methodology may be to perform CT only if core temperature can be maintained.
\-----
Still, it is important to realise that cold itself being a stressor demands a stress response. WIm Hof talks about how it is an adrenaline raising experience  . Again, at the correct dose, this promotes resilience to stress, and at a high dose, you have a chronically elevated stress state (high glucocorticoids, poor blood sugar regulation, etc …)
\======================================================

Sounds logical to me.
Next question, how to approach CT when sick and temperature is higher.

\----------------------
What is shivering?
Body trying to maintain core temperature?
Ie, is core temperature in decline when shivering starts?
\----------------------

What to do to increase adrenaline reserves?
..
