### JanSz — 2020-12-11T19:54:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293802>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sperti-or-oral-supplements.25372/page-2#post-293798](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sperti-or-oral-supplements.25372/page-2#post-293798)

\[QU OTE="John Schumacher, post: 293798, member: 23768"\]
[@Charmane](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23192/) If you do try supplementing with D, please consider K2 as well; there is a synergy with D3 & K2

-   [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7406600/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7406600/)
-   [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7436967/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC7436967/)
-   [https://www.hindawi.com/journals/ije/2017/7454376/](https://www.hindawi.com/journals/ije/2017/7454376/)

We know there are a few handicaps to building our own "vitamin" D stores:

-   Time of year (season) & Lattitude

-   Too much melanization in our skin's pigment [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...callus-and-melanated-skin-pigmentation.25252/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-there-a-difference-between-the-term-solar-callus-and-melanated-skin-pigmentation.25252/)

-   Dysfunctional cholesterol sulfation metabolism [https://academic.oup.com/edrv/article/36/5/526/2354688](https://academic.oup.com/edrv/article/36/5/526/2354688)

A note about light on you normally covered parts. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) makes a clear case for getting your breast in the sun [https://optimalklubs.com/reality22-sunlight-codes-for-darkness/](https://optimalklubs.com/reality22-sunlight-codes-for-darkness/)

So how can you supplement that in Oklahoma in the winter? Please consider UV mercury reptile bulbs with IR-A bulbs; you want both the UV plus IR-A together. Recommendation:

-   [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01855YCXM](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01855YCXM)
-   [https://www.amazon.com/Mega-Ray-Mercury-Vapor-Bulb-Watts/dp/B00TQG6DRI/](https://www.amazon.com/Mega-Ray-Mercury-Vapor-Bulb-Watts/dp/B00TQG6DRI/)

-   [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07DFDXZ1S](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B07DFDXZ1S)

I pair up one UV with one one IR using the adjustable lamp fixtures above (very stable).
These paired up on a wall 3 feet behind me.
So I have one set of lights pointing at me from two angles
There's enough more to "do the hokey pokey" and turn myself around, do squats, lunges, etc.
[View attachment 17183](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/17183/)

The bottom line in the diagram is the wall; the arrows are the angle the lights are pointing; the circle is where I stand at my desk.

[@Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) asked a very important question: I see your post came in at 11:29pm.

You know you need to improve your hormone "vitamin" D; however, how's your melatonin level?

-   Melatonin is the hormone of darkness [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4334454/ ->](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4334454/) The pineal gland receives input from postganglionic fibers, leading to the release of noradrenaline and increased production of cyclic AMP, thus activating the enzyme AANAT, mentioned above, which is critical to the production of melatonin.

-   Circadian rhythm is tightly coupled to our SCN response to sunlight [https://optimalklubs.com/reality22-sunlight-codes-for-darkness/](https://optimalklubs.com/reality22-sunlight-codes-for-darkness/)

-   Longer exposure to darkness leads to the increase of serum melatonin [https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2319417016000238 -](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2319417016000238) _not supplementation._

-   Melatonin can be used for the prevention and treatment of all cancers [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5503661/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5503661/)

-   Melatonin can be supplemented; however the best way is to use precursors for building your own melatonin; these are:
    -   tryptophan,
    -   methionine,
    -   inositol,
    -   magnesium -> Your best magnesium is _Magnesium L-Threonate_ because it can get passed your blood-brain-barrier.
    -   vitamins: B-2, B6 & D
    -   The **_inhibitor_** is any form of _Folic Acid_

-   Melatonin and Female Reproduction [https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fendo.2020.00085/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fendo.2020.00085/full)
    -   Please note the precursors mentioned in the article: serotonin & tryptophan

-   [https://www.janhealth.com/the-dance-of-hormones-and-neurotransmitters/](https://www.janhealth.com/the-dance-of-hormones-and-neurotransmitters/)
    -   Please note the reference to the neurotransmitter GABA

Now since you mentioned female hormones, there are two strategies you may want to consider: supplements and nutrition.

-   Wild Yam - [https://drhyman.com/downloads/Lifecyles-of-Women.pdf](https://drhyman.com/downloads/Lifecyles-of-Women.pdf)
-   [https://www.nationalnutrition.ca/articles/supplements/wild-yam/](https://www.nationalnutrition.ca/articles/supplements/wild-yam/)
-   Evening Primrose - [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6718646/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6718646/)

Grandpa John\[/QUOTE\]

### stanzi — 2020-12-12T00:54:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293807>

Hi Guys,

I will introduce myself by saying “this is rad” and “help” at the same time. =)

The “this is rad” part: I searched the Kruse forums for “low cortisol” and I found this thread. I’ve just started reading your discussions from five years ago in 2015. You guys might not even remember this but it was way back when JanSz and others were trying to help Eddie Garza with his low cortisol problem which is similar to mine, back in October 2015. I’ve read the first 33 out of 146 pages of this thread and this is genuinely the coolest and most informative discussion I’ve ever read! And I have only gotten to the year 2016 in this thread.

The “help” part: I am 3 weeks new to the Jack Kruse world. I have read Epi-Paleo Rx and that’s about it. The reason I’m here is I am a health nut/nerd, and I have a high stress job that I feel burnt out from. I’ve done 3 separate salivary cortisol tests and am low all day, with 6am=1.5ng/mL, Noon=1.2ng/mL, 4pm=1.4ng/mL, 9pm=0.5ng/mL. So, for me, low cortisol and I am sleeping poorly with early morning.

Full disclosure: I am a novice to the world of Kruse, so I would really really appreciate feedback from all. I’d be so grateful for that. Due to COVID, I am deferring getting the necessary labs for a few months until the pandemic has (hopefully) died down. In the meantime, I have made the following changes for ONLY THE LAST 3 WEEKS, as these are the big takeaways I’ve gathered from reading the first 30 pages of this post and from reading Epi-Paleo RX:

1) Sunset: When sun naturally sets, I now block all blue light (using blue blockers from lowbluelights.com) and try to avoid light in general. I found a really cool hack for my iPhone that says it removes all color spectrums except red. Not sure if it works as advertised but my screen looks red once I click on that feature. Since it is winter in Philadelphia (Latitude=40) I try to avoid light after the 5pm sunset.

2) Bedtime: I fall asleep at 9pm each night, right away with no problem. I wake up anywhere in the 3am to 5am range. Not by choice. I’d love to always wake up at 5am regularly. When I wake up, I now put on my blue blockers and don’t remove them until 730am when the sun naturally rises.

3) Breakfast: I now eat a BAB each morning, within 30 minutes of waking up. Since I wake up early, I am typically eating this breakfast at 530am with my blue blockers on.

4) Sunrise: At sunrise, I take my blue blockers off and take a 30-minute walk without my contact lenses in, to let my eyes catch some early morning sun. This is right at sunrise so I assume that this exposure is absent any UV.

5) Morning: I also now try to take a 30-minute walk sometime in the 10am to Noon timeframe. I assume that this should get me some UV and IR exposure.

6) Noon: Around noon each day, I now stand in the grass barefoot for 5 minutes. I think Dr. Kruse said the sun charges the earth with electrons, or something along those lines.

7) Food: I’ve added eggs, oysters, salmon, and mackerel to my diet. Formerly vegan (because I assumed it was healthiest). My diet now is mainly eggs, oysters, salmon, mackerel, veggies, avocado, healthy fats like olive oil, nuts, and seeds. I have made an effort to cut down on carbs since it is winter, and carbs=blue light in the winter.

8) Supplements: The only supplements I take are 20mg DHEA, 5mg sublingual Hydrocortisone, Magnesium (6 tablets MagSRT), a B vitamin complex (Klaire Labs Methyl Balance), and Longevenix Resveratrol. The DHEA and Hydrocortisone are pretty much “micro doses” but are for my low cortisol. With the 20mg of DHEA my salivary DHEA comes in at 6.8ng/mL (formerly 3.0ng/mL prior to supplementation). I have debated taking pregnenolone, but have deferred for now.

9) Melatonin: I have been taking a micro dose of 0.5mcg each night before bed, time released (brand REMfresh). Unclear if I should I continue or stop.

10) Health: I feel healthy other than feeling stressed from work and not sleeping optimally (early wakeups are killer). I am in good shape, at 6’1” and 175 pounds. I am 43 years old. I have good energy each day.

11) Work: I am in the consulting profession so I (unavoidably for now) spend 10 hours each day in front of a computer. I’ve recently ordered the Asus Low Blue “Eye Care” monitor but haven’t tried it out yet.

12) Water: We have a whole house Cuzn carbon and fluoride filter.

13) Internet: I bought a timer plug so that my WIFI automatically shuts off for 10 hours each night.

14) Iris computer monitor software, set to 2400K all day and 0K at the natural sunset.

15) Wine: One glass of merlot per evening. I realize this isn’t “optimal” but…

**The big question I have are: (1) Am I doing anything wrong above, and (2) Am I missing any big ticket items?**

Also, back in 2015 you guys were heavily discussing using reptile lights for UV and IR exposure. I think JanSz was using a couple Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-160W at a distance of 36 inches. Are you guys still doing that during the day? Has it made a difference now that it theoretically would be 5 years since you started that? Does everyone still think using these are safe for the eyes? If recommended, does the Solar-Glo have the appropriate infrared spectrum or does it need to be paired with a Philips Infrared R40 250W heat lamp or something similar? What about blacklight? Did you guys ever land on “the best” bulb combination for optimal artificial UV and IR exposure? Like something I could use from 9am to Noon to get as many benefits from the sun as possible (especially in the winter months).

**Great to meet everyone and be part of the community and I am excitedly awaiting some feedback from the pros. And, of course, many thanks in advance!**

Hi JanSz,

Thanks for the quick response and info on DDW. I’ve never heard of it but can’t wait to read up on it.

One thing I was wondering in the meantime is when you use your 2 Thermabulb IR and 2 Mega-Ray UV, it looks like you position them behind you at 30 inches away.

Why did you choose to put them behind you? Perhaps a silly question but don’t the eyes need UV too, to store up melatonin and optimize dopamine, among other things? If they are behind your head then can your eyes still absorb the UV rays?

### Dan2 — 2020-12-14T04:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293865>

[@stanzi](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25213/)

About the UV lights:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sperti-or-oral-supplements.25372/#post-293618](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/sperti-or-oral-supplements.25372/#post-293618)
(and the links to other threads in that post)
I've used the Sperti Fiji without the green-tinted protective goggles (like little swimming goggles), facing it eyes open for about an hour as a test of how necessary is the eye protection really with the intensity of UV from the bulbs.. experiment result data was eyeballs no bueno for several hours after. If you have a high enough ceiling to put the bulbs at an angle to the eyeballs similar to the angle the sun would be at when it's strong UV, it might be ok without goggles then, but with 8 foot ceilings you don't want those angles. I guess you could lay on your back and put the bulbs behind and above your head so the angle is high from the eyes, and be able to read something toward your belly like that. I use it on my back if I'm reading something or close my eyes and listen to something while facing it. You're right that the UV in the eyes signals for things, but with sunlight the UVB gradually increases as the angle above the eyes increases (and same with UVA before that). So to imitate that with indoor lightbulbs you could use a UVA blacklight before a UVB bulb (and the UVA is gentler on the eyes at lower angles), and then when you use the UVB, because the bulb(s) will go to full intensity suddenly (compared to gradual sunlight increase), you could make the angle from the eyes as much as you can and let it enter the eyes for a short time so there's some for the signaling while doing minimal eye damage. There have been some posts about how long does light need to enter the eyes to signal for different things and I think it depends on the wavelength and what it's signaling. I don't know how long for UVB signals. So with UVB bulbs, to avoid eye damage, prioritize the high angle and try that for times you cautiously increase.

\_\_\_

"7) Food: I’ve added eggs, oysters, salmon, and mackerel to my diet. Formerly vegan (because I assumed it was healthiest). My diet now is mainly eggs, oysters, salmon, mackerel, veggies, avocado, _healthy fats like olive oil, nuts, and seeds._ I have made an effort to cut down on carbs since it is winter, and carbs=blue light in the winter."

Might be some noticeable room for improvement there:

[https://thebigfatsurprise.com/](https://thebigfatsurprise.com/)

[https://www.youtube.com/c/PaulSaladinoMD/search?query=pufa](https://www.youtube.com/c/PaulSaladinoMD/search?query=pufa)

[https://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2020/12/13/linoleic-acid-health-effects.aspx](https://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2020/12/13/linoleic-acid-health-effects.aspx)

And I don't mean this to say you should necessarily change the diet to this extreme, but the possibility and implications might be interesting to you:
[https://justmeat.co/](https://justmeat.co/)
[https://justmeat.co/wiki/](https://justmeat.co/wiki/)
[https://justmeat.co/peoples/](https://justmeat.co/peoples/)

> 8) Supplements: The only supplements I take are 20mg DHEA, 5mg sublingual Hydrocortisone, Magnesium (6 tablets MagSRT), a B vitamin complex (Klaire Labs Methyl Balance), and Longevenix Resveratrol. The DHEA and Hydrocortisone are pretty much “micro doses” but are for my low cortisol. With the 20mg of DHEA my salivary DHEA comes in at 6.8ng/mL (formerly 3.0ng/mL prior to supplementation). I have debated taking pregnenolone, but have deferred for now.
>
> 9) Melatonin: I have been taking a micro dose of 0.5mcg each night before bed, time released (brand REMfresh). Unclear if I should I continue or stop.
>
> 10) Health: I feel healthy other than feeling stressed from work and not sleeping optimally (early wakeups are killer). I am in good shape, at 6’1” and 175 pounds. I am 43 years old. I have good energy each day.
>
> 11) Work: I am in the consulting profession so I (unavoidably for now) spend 10 hours each day in front of a computer. I’ve recently ordered the Asus Low Blue “Eye Care” monitor but haven’t tried it out yet.
>
> 12) Water: We have a whole house Cuzn carbon and fluoride filter.
>
> 13) Internet: I bought a timer plug so that my WIFI automatically shuts off for 10 hours each night.
>
> 14) Iris computer monitor software, set to 2400K all day and 0K at the natural sunset.
>
> 15) Wine: One glass of merlot per evening. I realize this isn’t “optimal” but…
>
>
> **The big question I have are: (1) Am I doing anything wrong above, and (2) Am I missing any big ticket items?**
>
>
> Also, back in 2015 you guys were heavily discussing using reptile lights for UV and IR exposure. I think JanSz was using a couple Exo-Terra-Solar-Glo-160W at a distance of 36 inches. Are you guys still doing that during the day? Has it made a difference now that it theoretically would be 5 years since you started that? Does everyone still think using these are safe for the eyes? If recommended, does the Solar-Glo have the appropriate infrared spectrum or does it need to be paired with a Philips Infrared R40 250W heat lamp or something similar? What about blacklight? Did you guys ever land on “the best” bulb combination for optimal artificial UV and IR exposure? Like something I could use from 9am to Noon to get as many benefits from the sun as possible (especially in the winter months).
>
>
> **Great to meet everyone and be part of the community and I am excitedly awaiting some feedback from the pros. And, of course, many thanks in advance!**

How is your libido?

### stanzi — 2020-12-14T20:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293875>

[@caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)

Thanks for wanting to help me. My libido is definitely noticeably lower than it was a few years ago, although not non existent. I've not been able to do any blood testing thus far, only salivary testing from a company named "Health Confirm" that tested Cortisol (morning, noon, evening, bedtime), DHEA-S (morning), Estradiol, Progesterone, and Testosterone. Again, all salivary. Most recent results are:

· Cortisol (morning) = 1.5 ng/mL with normal range 3.7-9.5

· Cortisol (noon) = 1.2 ng/mL with normal range 1.2-3.0

· Cortisol (evening) = 1.4 ng/mL with normal range 0.6-3.9

· Cortisol (night) = 0.5 ng/mL with normal range 0.4-1.0

· Estradiol (E2) = 0.8 pg/mL with normal range 0.5-2.2

· Progesterone (Pg) =33 with normal range 12-100

· Free Testosterone = 525 pg/mL with normal range 44-148

· DHEA-S = 5.3 with normal range 2-23 ng/mL and 10mg of supplemental DHEA taken in the morning for a full two months before this test

### stanzi — 2020-12-14T20:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293876>

[@Dan2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/20918/)

Thanks much for all the extra info. I've spent the last 3 hours doing an initial read through of all the links you pointed me to. It is \*a lot\* to digest, but I hope you’ll be so kind as to let me run a few questions by you. As my goal is to only “supplement” the natural sun during the two winter months where natural UV isn’t available from the sun, the Chapiro LED options seem a bit pricey for my intended use. Do you mind giving this a read through to make sure that I am understanding the setup you’re using?

1) Sunrise (730am in my Latitude): Use Red + IR + 2 Edison incandescent for 20-30 minutes

2) Morning (9am-10am sun): Add UVA Blacklight to the Red + IR + Incandescent bulb mix

3) Midday Sun (Noon): Swap out UVA Blacklight for Sperti Fiji. Keep Red, IR, and Incandescent bulbs running. This would provide UVC, UVB, UVA, and heavy visible red and IR

4) Setup: When you use this, you are standing up and facing a bookcase that is 4 feet in front of you, where the Sperti is positioned on a shelf with its center at waist level, with the Red / IR / Incandescent / UVA Blacklights positioned on the top shelf of the bookcase, where the top shelf is closer to the 8 feet high ceiling height.

If I have this correct (BIG if), then I have a few clarifying questions:

1) Can you please confirm that I have the “bookcase” configuration correctly explained above?

2) For each of the “sunrise”, “morning”, and “midday” simulations defined above, are you using protective goggles? If not, are your eyes opened or closed? I think that I read with the “midday” Sperti setup, you’re not wearing protective goggles but are closing your eyes? What about when the Sperti isn’t being used (like for "sunrise" and "morning"), are you relaxing with your eyes closed or trying to get some work done?

3) I see that you’re doing the “sunrise” simulation for 20-30 minutes. How about for the “morning” and “midday” simulations, how long are you usually doing each of these for, again, given that you're standing ~4 feet from the bulbs?

4) What wattage Incandescent are you using? 150W is the strongest I can find on Amazon. And two bulbs, correct?

5) What red bulb are you using? Is it still the Exo-Terra Daytime Heat Lamp (link below)?
[http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/daytime\_heat\_lamp.php](http://www.exo-terra.com/en/products/daytime_heat_lamp.php)

6) What IR bulb are you using? On Amazon I found this RubyLux NIR-A Near Infrared Bulb? I believe it is 250W and the company says “Our Near Infrared Bulb's frequency range is between 704-1016nm and It peaks strongly at around 850nm. 87% is in the 800-1000nm range”.
[https://rubyluxlights.com/products/rubylux-near-infrared-bulb-grade-a](https://rubyluxlights.com/products/rubylux-near-infrared-bulb-grade-a)

7) For the UVA Blacklight, I’ve read a lot of discussion about what spectrum this should ideally include. And if it should it stop at 380? 400? Other? Can you please share with me what UVA Blacklight you finally decided to use? I see that the Duracell one that Dr. Kruse uses is no longer available.

8) In Jack’s “Time #2” series, I see that the eyes need to be exposed to pre sunrise early morning blue light exposure to properly sync the circadian rhythm for the entire day. How (if at all) are you handling this? I’ve seen JanSz previously recommend using a Philips GoLite BLU lamp right when first waking up (I think this is what JanSz recommended a while back).
[https://www.usa.philips.com/c-p/HF3429\_60/golite-blu-energy-light](https://www.usa.philips.com/c-p/HF3429_60/golite-blu-energy-light)

9) Just to confirm, this is the Sperti Fiji that you're using, correct?
[https://www.sperti.com/product/fiji-sun-tanning-lamp/](https://www.sperti.com/product/fiji-sun-tanning-lamp/)

My apologies in advance for the barrage of questions. As my cortisol curve is low and flat all day, and my sleep isn’t optimal (early morning 3am wakeups are regular for me), and my dopamine is probably low too (based on Dr. Kruse’s dopamine and Purple light blog), I really want to make sure I do the best I can to remedy all this in the winter, when using the sun is difficult. Again, many thanks.

### Dan2 — 2020-12-15T03:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293882>

_1) Sunrise (730am in my Latitude): Use Red + IR + 2 Edison incandescent for 20-30 minutes_

Yep

_2) Morning (9am-10am sun): Add UVA Blacklight to the Red + IR + Incandescent bulb mix_

Yep

_3) Midday Sun (Noon): Swap out UVA Blacklight for Sperti Fiji. Keep Red, IR, and Incandescent bulbs running. This would provide UVC, UVB, UVA, and heavy visible red and IR_

Yep

_4) Setup: When you use this, you are standing up and facing a bookcase that is 4 feet in front of you, where the Sperti is positioned on a shelf with its center at waist level, with the Red / IR / Incandescent / UVA Blacklights positioned on the top shelf of the bookcase, where the top shelf is closer to the 8 feet high ceiling height.

1) Can you please confirm that I have the “bookcase” configuration correctly explained above?_

I would put the Fiji highest if I could, for the higher angle of UVB to the eyes, more like the angle when the sunlight has UVB, but I don't have space between the bookcase and ceiling to put the Fiji that high sitting on top of the bookcase, so it's at waist level on a chair angled upward a little. The other bulbs are all arranged at about waist to head level with the position of each being so it blends the colors balanced on my skin, and the light from those meet where the main intensity of the Fiji spectrum is shining to when it's angled up some. If you use clamp lights, taking off the circular reflector part lets you put the bulbs closer together without the reflector overlapping with the light from nearby bulbs and blocking it some.

I didn't mean the bookcase setup is what everyone should do; it's just what I had to clamp the clamp lights onto. You could also maybe use a coat rack with a heavy base or a gardening trellis or?

_2) For each of the “sunrise”, “morning”, and “midday” simulations defined above, are you using protective goggles? If not, are your eyes opened or closed? I think that I read with the “midday” Sperti setup, you’re not wearing protective goggles but are closing your eyes? What about when the Sperti isn’t being used (like for "sunrise" and "morning"), are you relaxing with your eyes closed or trying to get some work done?_

I don't use the goggles when the Fiji is on and I'm facing it because I don't want to get tanlines like a racoon. So yeah if I'm facing the Fiji I close my eyes and listen to something. When it's any bulb other than UVB -- with the possible exception of a UVA bulb stronger than a CFL blacklight, which might be damaging to the eyes (I'm not sure; I just use one CFL blacklight) -- I feel comfortable and don't notice any eye damage from having my open eyes facing the other kinds of bulbs. It might not be good to look too close to the 250W infrared heat lamps for too long, but I think just having a little angle of the eyes away from the center of those is enough for it to be safe for at least a few hours a day.

### Dan2 — 2020-12-15T03:39:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293883>

edit: It might be more convenient for you to get a meter to measure the intensity at wavelengths, so you can use a combination of bulbs that's close to the UV through far-IR wavelength proportions of sun, instead of trying to make sense of my reasoning that I did because of trying to save money.
I don't know if there's one meter that can do that though (UV through far-IR).
[https://www.calex.co.uk/site/wp-content/uploads/2015/07/understanding-and-using-ir.pdf](https://www.calex.co.uk/site/wp-content/uploads/2015/07/understanding-and-using-ir.pdf)
[https://www.thomassci.com/scientific-supplies/Uv-Measuring-Meter](https://www.thomassci.com/scientific-supplies/Uv-Measuring-Meter)
_
3) I see that you’re doing the “sunrise” simulation for 20-30 minutes. How about for the “morning” and “midday” simulations, how long are you usually doing each of these for, again, given that you're standing ~4 feet from the bulbs?_

The mid-morning combination that has UVA but not UVB is safe to do for however long would be within the couple hours that'd be in sunlight at a warmer time of year. For the midday spectrum, I go by how my skin feels from the UVB, so it depends how much tolerance to it you have. Standing about 3-4 feet from the Fiji (with the infrared+red and visible bulbs too to improve the skin's handling of the UVB) I was able to use it for about 10 minutes the first time (middle of winter when I had no tan) before my skin got a little irritated, then increased that within the first week to about 20 or 30 minutes, and if I've been using it regularly could comfortably use it for about 2-3 hours.

My skin color is like from northwestern Europe. I stand farther from the Fiji not just because of the infrared heat lamps at its same distance making a strong magnetic field; also because of reasoning that at the latitude my genetics are adapted to (about 50 degrees north), it means I can be outside in the strongest of the year summer sun UVB as it increases through the middle of the day and not get sunburned if I've gradually built up to that as the seasons warm, and that's about three or four hours of strong (for me) UVB; but if I stand say a foot or two from the Fiji, I'll start to get irritated skin in less time than that, meaning the Fiji intensity at the recommended distance in the manual (18") is stronger UVB than would naturally be at the latitude my skin's adapted to. So I stand farther back so the intensity is more like middle summer 50 degrees north intensity, estimating where it's that intensity by how long before it starts to irritate my skin like a pre-burn.

But another thing with the Fiji is that at that distance where I can use it for a few hours before pre-burn irritation, I also don't get as tan as I would if I were in summer sun for that amount of time (also without getting burned), so there's something lacking (or in excess) in the Fiji plus other bulbs setup compared to sun. It might be the proportions of wavelengths, meaning putting the lights at different distances would fix it. Or it might be a lack of something, I'm guessing maybe far-infrared 3000 nm + wavelengths that the infrared heat bulbs don't make but there's a little of in sunlight. Or could be both of those.

I experimented with the combinations of bulbs for the wavelength proportions by starting with the Fiji then adding more warm visible color bulbs because the Fiji looks too pale blue compared to sunlight, and added two 250W infrared heat lamps to try to make the warmth similar to the warmth of summer sun (balancing the IR's role with the UVB) which also add a lot more visible red than is in sunlight. Maybe I get skin irritation from the Fiji (3-4 feet away) in the same number of hours as I would from strongest summer sun but without as much of a tan because of the infrared from the bulbs setup not being enough; maybe the UVB of the Fiji at that distance is about what the UVB would be at 50 degrees north in summer, but the infrared is proportionally less, and that's affecting the tanning.

I only use two 250W heat lamps because of trying to not have too much of a magnetic field from those, but at the 3-4 feet distance it's not nearly as hot as summer sun. That could also be because of a lack of far-infrared. I'm not sure how much near-infrared and far-infrared are perceived as heat compared to each other, and so if I added far-infrared to the two 250W heat lamps (which are mostly near-IR) if it would feel more hot like summer sun. It could be both too little near-infrared because of the heat lamps being too far compared to the Fiji and very little far-infrared.

If it's too little near-IR proportional to UVB, putting the heat lamps closer than the Fiji could make the near-IR proportional to UVB more like summer sun, but I haven't done that (because of them being clamped onto a bookcase the Fiji is in front of and the space in the room being only far enough for the Fiji to be like 50 degrees north summer sun if I have the Fiji the same distance up against the bookcase). Strong IR LEDs could be a solution for more near-IR with less magnetic field, but the power from LEDs needed for the heat to be like summer (even 50 degrees north summer) gets expensive.

### JanSz — 2020-12-21T14:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-293971>

[Qiaochu Yuan](https://www.quora.com/profile/Qiaochu-Yuan-1)
·
[April 10, 2014](https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-significance-of-SU-3-in-physics/answer/Qiaochu-Yuan-1)
ex-PhD student in math
[What is the significance of SU(3) in physics?](https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-significance-of-SU-3-in-physics)
Probably the most important fact about physics that you wouldn't learn in a first or even a second course (at least, I didn't), and that you wouldn't learn in a popular physics book either (at least, I didn't), is [Noether's theorem](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noether's_theorem)

. Noether's theorem says, roughly speaking, that any continuous symmetry of the laws of physics gives rise to a conservation law. For example:

-   Translational symmetry (the laws of physics look the same if I walk forward, backward, left, right...) gives rise to conservation of momentum.
-   Time symmetry (the laws of physics look the same at all times) gives rise to conservation of energy.
-   Rotational symmetry (the laws of physics look the same if I spin around) gives rise to conservation of angular momentum.

Now, all of the symmetries I described above have the property that they're symmetries of spacetime. But there are some important symmetries giving rise to important conservation laws that don't arise in this way. For example, in electromagnetism, charge is also conserved, but what could the corresponding symmetry be?

The symmetry giving rise to charge conservation is an example of a gauge symmetry ([Gauge theory](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gauge_theory)

); very roughly speaking, that means it isn't a spacetime symmetry like above. The corresponding group ([Group theory](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Group_theory)), namely the group U(1)" role="presentation">U(1)
of unit complex numbers, is an example of a gauge group. Theories with gauge symmetries are called gauge theories, and it turns out that electromagnetism in particular can be described as a gauge theory.

The Standard Model of particle physics is also a gauge theory, but with more complicated gauge symmetry; its gauge group is U(1)&#x00D7;SU(2)&#x00D7;SU(3)" role="presentation">U(1)×SU(2)×SU(3)

, where the first group is related to electromagnetism, the second group is related to the weak nuclear force, and the third group - the one you're asking about - is related to the strong nuclear force; that's why it's significant.

I don't know enough physics to be confident what the corresponding conserved quantities coming from SU(2)" role="presentation">SU(2)

and SU(3)" role="presentation">SU(3) are, but I think they are weak isospin ([Weak isospin](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Weak_isospin)) and color charge ([Color charge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Color_charge)
) respectively; someone who knows better can correct me.

See also:

-   [Quantum electrodynamics](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_electrodynamics)

(what happens when you only consider U(1)" role="presentation">U(1)

-   )

-   [Quantum chromodynamics](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_chromodynamics)

(what happens when you only consider SU(3)" role="presentation">SU(3))

### JanSz — 2021-01-02T20:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-294278>

[Gift tax - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gift_tax)
[Gift tax in the United States - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gift_tax_in_the_United_States)
[Poland - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poland)
**Noncitizen spouse**
**[https://www.lawyerspoland.eu/inheritance-tax-in-poland](https://www.lawyerspoland.eu/inheritance-tax-in-poland)**
**17 States With Estate or Inheritance Taxes**
[https://www.aarp.org/money/taxes/info-2020/states-with-estate-inheritance-taxes.html](https://www.aarp.org/money/taxes/info-2020/states-with-estate-inheritance-taxes.html)

-   **Connecticut:** Estate tax of 10 percent to 12 percent on estates above $5.1 million

-   **District of Columbia:** Estate tax of 12 percent to 16 percent on estates above $5.8 million
-   **Hawaii:** Estate tax of 10 percent to 20 percent on estates above $5.5 million
-   **Illinois:** Estate tax of 0.8 percent to 16 percent on estates above $4 million
-   **Iowa:** Inheritance tax of up to 15 percent
-   **Kentucky:** Inheritance tax of up to 16 percent
-   **Maine:** Estate tax of 8 percent to 12 percent on estates above $5.7 million
-   **Maryland:** Estate tax of 0.8 percent to 16 percent on estates above $5 million; inheritance tax of up to 10 percent
-   **Massachusetts:** 0.8 percent to 16 percent on estates above $1 million
-   **Minnesota:** 13 percent to 16 percent on estates above $3 million
-   **Nebraska:** Inheritance tax of up to 18 percent
-   **New Jersey:** Inheritance tax of up to 16 percent
-   **New York:** Estate tax of 3.06 percent to 16 percent for estates above $5.9 million
-   **Oregon:** Estate tax of 10 percent to 16 percent on estates above $1 million
-   **Pennsylvania:** Inheritance tax of up to 15 percent
-   **Rhode Island:** Estate tax of 0.8 percent to 16 percent on estates above $1.6 million
-   **Vermont:** Estate tax of 16 percent on estates above $2.8 million
-   **Washington:** Estate tax of 10 percent to 20 percent on estates above $2.2 million

\===============================================================
New Jersey does not assess an intangible personal property tax, but it does impose an [inheritance tax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inheritance_tax).

### JanSz — 2021-01-02T21:16:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-294279>

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inheritance\_tax](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inheritance_tax)

-   [New Jersey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_Jersey): New Jersey law puts **inheritors** into different groups, based on their family relationship to the deceased person:
    -   **Class A** **beneficiaries** are exempt from the inheritance tax. They includes the **deceased** person's spouse, domestic partner, or civil union partner parent, grandparent, child (biological, adopted, or mutually acknowledged), stepchild (but not stepgrandchild or great-stepgrandchild), grandchild or other lineal descendant of a child
    -   **Class B** was deleted when New Jersey law changed
    -   **Class C** includes the deceased person's: brother or sister, spouse or [civil union](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Same-sex_unions_in_the_United_States) partner of the deceased person's child, surviving spouse or civil union partner of the deceased person's child. The first $25,000 inherited by someone in Class C is not taxed. On amounts exceeding $25,000, the tax rates are: 11% on the next $1,075,000, 13% on the next $300,000, 14% on the next $300,000, and 16% for anything over $1,700,000
    -   **Class D** includes everyone else. There is no special exemption amount, and the applicable tax rates are: 15% on the first $700,000, and 16% on anything over $700,000
    -   **Class E** includes the State of New Jersey or any of its political subdivisions for public or charitable purposes, an educational institution, church, hospital, orphan asylum, [public library](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_libraries_in_North_America), and other nonprofits. These beneficiaries are exempt from inheritance tax.

[@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) Saw you had low cortisol before too. Did you experience any post exercise crashes at any point? Ive stopped any moderate exercise for about a year, minus a couple of times to test if I crash still. All the doctors I have seen have been pretty stumped with what's going on. I'm improving in all other areas, just haven't been able to get improvement in this area yet.

### JanSz — 2021-02-11T15:07:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-295431>

[
Viktor T. Toth](https://www.quora.com/profile/Viktor-T-Toth-1)
·
[January 27](https://www.quora.com/If-two-photons-are-entangled-If-the-wavelength-of-one-photon-changes-is-the-wavelength-of-the-other-affected/answer/Viktor-T-Toth-1)
IT pro, part-time physicist
[If two photons are entangled, If the wavelength of one photon changes is the wavelength of the other affected?](https://www.quora.com/If-two-photons-are-entangled-If-the-wavelength-of-one-photon-changes-is-the-wavelength-of-the-other-affected)

Allow me to respond to this question by first clarifying two common misconceptions.

First, when we speak of entangled particles, what we really mean is that we created a set of particles that, at least temporarily, are not entangled with everything else. Because that is the normal state of things: everything is entangled with everything else almost all the time. In these laboratory experiments, we manage to isolate, e.g., a pair of photons from everything else in the world, so that we can study entanglement under such artificially created, “clean” circumstances without the unpredictable, random environment.

Second, the wavelength of a photon is not an intrinsic property. It is observer-dependent. An observer that is running after the photon will see a longer wavelength. An observer that is running in the opposite direction will see a shorter wavelength.

OK, with that in mind… if the photon’s wavelength changes because an observer changes reference frames (e.g., a previously stationary observer begins to move) than obviously, the other photon’s wavelength would change the same way. They do not even need to be entangled; what changed, after all, is not the photons but the observer’s reference frame.

If the photon’s wavelength changes because it interacts with its environment (e.g., it enters a refractive medium) then it really is no longer entangled only with its counterpart. There is now a complex interaction between this photon and the environment that caused its wavelength to change. As such, the “pure” entanglement (involving only the two photons, with the environment excluded) is broken, so we would no longer describe the pair as entangled.

_Biomagnetic fluid dynamics (BFD) takes into accounts both magnetization and electrical conductivity of blood. It is essential to couple the ferrohydrodynamic (FHD) and magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) principles when magnetic fields are applied to blood as a biomagnetic fluid._

Two-Phase Biofluid Flow Model for Magnetic Drug Targeting - [https://www.mdpi.com/2073-8994/12/7/1083/pdf](https://www.mdpi.com/2073-8994/12/7/1083/pdf)

Nanoparticle-mediated drug delivery systems (DDS) appear to be a novel approach for drug delivery to diseased parts of the vasculature system. The most commonly used drug nanocarriers for the MNPs are protein molecules, peptides, and deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA). Targeting agents and permeation enhancers are mounted on the surface of MNPs to improve the therapeutic outcome. For atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease, the therapeutic goal is to reduce the likelihood of myocardial infraction and stabilize the atherosclerotic plaque.

### JanSz — 2021-03-07T18:49:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-296096>

[https://www.thunderbolts.info/wp/2021/02/23/the-long-farewell-to-the-big-bang-model-of-cosmology/](https://www.thunderbolts.info/wp/2021/02/23/the-long-farewell-to-the-big-bang-model-of-cosmology/)

The cosmos is to be understood as an open system, not as an inflating ball.
\-----
Dirac even spoke in this context of God, whom he considered being a “highly brilliant mathematician.” Wolfgang Pauli once put it: “Our friend Dirac has a religion, and its motto is: There is no God, and Dirac is his prophet.”
\----------
The Dirac equations of quantum mechanics are based on the special theory of relativity, the basis of which is the Lorentz transformation of Maxwell’s equations, whose sole purpose for Einstein was to symmetrize these equations. This symmetrization is a physically completely nonsensical operation since it destroys the character of the equations, namely to describe the propagation of electromagnetic waves.
\-----------
As a second “stroke of genius,” Albert Einstein invented the **curvature of space** through gravity because he could not explain how celestial bodies were kept in their orbit. Einstein himself must have taken it as a joke because he confessed to his friend Paul Ehrenfest: “I’ve already done something wrong in the theory of gravity, which puts me at risk of being interned in a madhouse.” 4 Only anyone who knows something about physics and geometry knows that **curvatures can only be observed on surface**s and that light rays are influenced in their direction by diffraction due to optical effects.

Whole generations of physicists seem to have no longer learned the **difference between a surface and a space.** Physically, a surface presents itself as a phase boundary between two different states of matter.
\-------------------------
One of the most spectacular examples of this **alternative facts** is the black hole, which repeatedly stimulates people’s fantasies.
\------------------------
This dispute continued for many years and ended in 2014 with Hawking’s admission **that there were no black holes.**7 Notwithstanding this, the 2020 Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded _“_for the discovery **that the formation of black holes** is a robust prediction of general relativity.” You rub your eyes in amazement.
\-------------------------

****Spacetime is space and time, not a four-dimensional space**.**

THE ROYAL INSTITUTION
The Physics of Magnetic Monopoles - with Felix Flicker
742,378 views

time 45:00
in 1831 Michael Faraday said:
What is the use of electricity and electromagnetism
What is the use of newborn child

electricity
magnetricity
electron=electric monopole----->>movement of electric charges=electric current
cannot have magnetic current because north pole (always) follows south pole
but you can have magnetic current (of magnetic monopoles) within spin ice

spintronic

switch from using charges to using spin
...
**Moore's law**
Moore's law is the observation that the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit doubles about every two years.
\-----
We already have single-atom transistors.
\---
Artificial spin ices.
\---
**Landauer's principle**
Landauer's principle is a physical principle pertaining to the lower theoretical limit of energy consumption of computation. It holds that "any logically irreversible manipulation of information, such as the erasure of a bit or the merging of two computation paths, must be accompanied by a corresponding entropy increase in non-information-bearing degrees of freedom of the information-processing apparatus or its environment".
\---

time 49:50
Last year's computer data service (of the world) used 40% more energy than the entire UK (United Kingdom).

////
MRI
huge magnetic field to cause spin flips
the reason that it must be so large is that coupling between field and those spins is very weak.
Alternatively, much smaller magnetic field could be used
if
much more sensitive detectors were available.
Paper of National Academy of Science on this topic is written (2016).

Early Signs of Adenovirus
a person insists that they are cold and is shivering but have a high body temperature

.................
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shivering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shivering)
Shivering and the elderly
The functional capacity of the thermoregulatory system alters with aging, reducing the resistance of elderly people to extreme external temperatures.
The shiver response may be greatly diminished or even absent in the elderly, resulting in a significant drop in mean deep body temperature upon exposure to cold. Standard tests of thermoregulatory function show a markedly different rate of decline of thermoregulatory processes in different individuals with ageing.\[4\]
\------
Newborn babies, infants, and young children experience a greater (net) heat loss than adults because they cannot shiver to maintain body heat\[citation needed\].
They rely on non-shivering thermogenesis.
Children have an increased amount of brown adipose tissue (increased vascular supply, and high mitochondrial density), and, when cold-stressed, will have greater oxygen consumption and will release norepinephrine.
Norepinephrine will react with lipases in brown fat to break down fat into triglycerides.
Triglycerides are then metabolized to glycerol and non-esterified fatty acids.
These are then further degraded in the needed heat-generating process to form CO2 and water.
Chemically, in mitochondria the proton gradient producing the proton electromotive force **that is ordinarily used to synthesize ATP is instead** bypassed to produce heat directly.

.......................
This part may be suspect or inaccurate or should be described in a different way.
**that is ordinarily used to synthesize ATP is instead** bypassed to produce heat directly.

When water is produced by mitochondria great amount of heat is generated.

If the body needs more heat, then more water should be synthesized.

### JanSz — 2021-03-24T19:37:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-296592>

[https://buoy.antville.org/stories/1466743/](https://buoy.antville.org/stories/1466743/)

Tuesday, 5. September 2006
Richard Feynman On Playing Samba

There was a man at the U.S. Embassy who knew I liked samba music. I
think I told him that when I had been in Brazil the first time, I had heard
a samba band practicing in the street, and I wanted to learn more about
Brazilian music.
He said a small group, called a regional, practiced at his apartment
every week, and I could come over and listen to them play.
There were three or four people -- one was the janitor from the
apartment house -- and they played rather quiet music up in his apartment;
they had no other place to play. One guy had a tambourine that they called a
pandeiro, and another guy had a small guitar. I kept hearing the beat of a
drum somewhere, but there was no drum! Finally I figured out that it was the
tambourine, which the guy was playing in a complicated way, twisting his
wrist and hitting the skin with his thumb. I found that interesting, and
learned how to play the pandeiro, more or less.
Then the season for Carnaval began to come around. That's the season
when new music is presented. They don't put out new music and records all
the time; they put them all out during Carnaval time, and it's very
exciting.
It turned out that the janitor was the composer for a small samba
"school" -- not a school in the sense of education, but in the sense of fish
\-- from Copacabana Beach, called Farqantes de Copacabana, which means
"Fakers from Copacabana," which was just right for me, and he invited me to
be in it.
Now this samba school was a thing where guys from the favelas -- the
poor sections of the city -- would come down, and meet behind a construction
lot where some apartment houses were being built, and practice the new music
for the Carnaval.
I chose to play a thing called a "frigideira," which is a toy frying
pan made of metal, about six inches in diameter, with a little metal stick
to beat it with. It's an accompanying instrument which makes a tinkly, rapid
noise that goes with the main samba music and rhythm and fills it out. So I
tried to play this thing and everything was going all right. We were
practicing, the music was roaring along and we were going like sixty, when
all of a sudden the head of the batteria section, a great big black man,
yelled out, "STOP! Hold it, hold it -- wait a minute!" And everybody
stopped. "Something's wrong with the frigideiras!" he boomed out. "O
Americana, outra vez!" ("The American again!")
So I felt uncomfortable. I practiced all the time. I'd walk along the
beach holding two sticks that I had picked up, getting the twisty motion of
the wrists, practicing, practicing, practicing. I kept working on it, but I
always felt inferior, that I was some kind of trouble, and wasn't really up
to it.
Well, it was getting closer to Carnaval time, and one evening there was
a conversation between the leader of the band and another guy, and then the
leader started coming around, picking people out: "You!" he said to a
trumpeter. "You!" he said to a singer. "You!" -- and he pointed to me. I
figured we were finished. He said, "Go out in front!"
We went out to the front of the construction site -- the five or six of
us -- and there was an old Cadillac convertible, with its top down. "Get
in!" the leader said.
There wasn't enough room for us all, so some of us had to sit up on the
back. I said to the guy next to me, "What's he doing -- is he putting us
out?"
"Nao se, nao se." ("I don't know.")
We drove off way up high on a road which ended near the edge of a cliff
overlooking the sea. The car stopped and the leader said, "Get out!" -- and
they walked us right up to the edge of the cliff!
And sure enough, he said, "Now line up! You first, you next, you next!
Start playing! Now march!"
We would have marched off the edge of the cliff -- except for a steep
trail that went down. So our little group goes down the trail -- the
trumpet, the singer, the guitar, the pandeiro, and the frigideira -- to an
outdoor party in the woods. We weren't picked out because the leader wanted
to get rid of us; he was sending us to this private party that wanted some
samba music! And afterwards he collected money to pay for some costumes for
our band.
After that I felt a little better, because I realized, that when he
picked the frigideira player, he picked me!
Another thing happened to increase my confidence. Some time later, a
guy came from another samba school, in Leblon, a beach further on. He wanted
to join our school.
The boss said, "Where're you from?"
"Leblon."
"What do you play?"
"Frigideira."
"OK. Let me hear you play the frigideira."
So this guy picked up his frigideira and his metal stick and...
"brrra-dup-dup; chick-a-chick." Gee whiz! It was wonderful!
The boss said to him, "You go over there and stand next to O Americana,
and you'll learn how to play the frigideira!"
My theory is that it's like a person who speaks French who comes to
America. At first they're making all kinds of mistakes, and you can hardly
understand them. Then they keep on practicing until they speak rather well,
and you find there's a delightful twist to their way of speaking -- their
accent is rather nice, and you love to listen to it. So I must have had some
sort of accent playing the frigideira, because I couldn't compete with those
guys who had been playing it all their lives; it must have been some kind of
dumb accent. But whatever it was, I became a rather successful frigideira
player.
One day, shortly before Carnaval time, the leader of the samba school
said, "OK, we're going to practice marching in the street."
We all went out from the construction site to the street, and it was
full of traffic. The streets of Copacabana were always a big mess. Believe
it or not, there was a trolley line in which the trolley cars went one way,
and the automobiles went the other way. Here it was rush hour in Copacabana,
and we were going to march down the middle of Avenida Atlantica.
I said to myself, "Jesus! The boss didn't get a license, he didn't OK
it with the police, he didn't do anything. He's decided we're just going to
go out."
So we started to go out into the street, and everybody, all around, was
excited. Some volunteers from a group of bystanders took a rope and formed a
big square around our band, so the pedestrians wouldn't walk through our
lines. People started to lean out of the windows. Everybody wanted to hear
the new samba music. It was very exciting!
As soon as we started to march, I saw a policeman, way down at the
other end of the road. He looked, saw what was happening, and started
diverting traffic! Everything was informal. Nobody made any arrangements,
but it worked fine. The people were holding the ropes around us, the
policeman was diverting the traffic, the pedestrians were crowded and the
traffic was jammed, but we were going along great! We walked down the
street, around the corners, and all over the damn Copacabana, at random!
Finally we ended up in a little square in front of the apartment where
the boss's mother lived. We stood there in this place, playing, and the
guy's mother, and aunt, and so on, came down. They had aprons on; they had
been working in the kitchen, and you could see their excitement -- they were
almost crying. It was really nice to do that human stuff. And all the people
leaning out of the windows -- that was terrific! And I remembered the time I
had been in Brazil before, and had seen one of these samba bands -- how I
loved the music and nearly went crazy over it -- and now I was in it!
By the way, when we were marching around the streets of Copacabana that
day, I saw in a group on the sidewalk two young ladies from the embassy.
Next week I got a note from the embassy saying, "It's a great thing you are
doing, yak, yak, yak..." as if my purpose was to improve relations between
the United States and Brazil! So it was a "great" thing I was doing.
Well, in order to go to these rehearsals, I didn't want to go dressed
in my regular clothes that I wore to the university. The people in the band
were very poor, and had only old, tattered clothes. So I put on an old
undershirt, some old pants, and so forth, so I wouldn't look too peculiar.
But then I couldn't walk out of my luxury hotel on Avenida Atlantica in
Copacabana Beach through the lobby. So I always took the elevator down to
the bottom and went out through the basement.
A short time before Carnaval, there was going to be a special
competition between the samba schools of the beaches -- Copacabana, Ipanema,
and Leblon; there were three or four schools, and we were one. We were going
to march in costume down Avenida Atlantica. I felt a little uncomfortable
about marching in one of those fancy Carnaval costumes, since I wasn't a
Brazilian. But we were supposed to be dressed as Greeks, so I figured I'm as
good a Greek as they are.
On the day of the competition, I was eating at the hotel restaurant,
and the head waiter, who had often seen me tapping on the table when there
was samba music playing, came over to me and said, "Mr. Feynman, this
evening there's going to be something you will love! It's tipico Brasileiro
\-- typical Brazilian: There's going to be a march of the samba schools right
in front of the hotel! And the music is so good -- you must hear it."
I said, "Well, I'm kind of busy tonight. I don't know if I can make
it."
"Oh! But you'd love it so much! You must not miss it! It's tipico
Brasileiro!"
He was very insistent, and as I kept telling him I didn't think I'd be
there to see it, he became disappointed.
That evening I put on my old clothes and went down through the
basement, as usual. We put on the costumes at the construction lot and began
marching down Avenida Atlantica, a hundred Brazilian Greeks in paper
costumes, and I was in the back, playing away on the frigideira.
Big crowds were along both sides of the Avenida; everybody was leaning
out of the windows, and we were coming up to the Miramar Hotel, where I was
staying. People were standing on the tables and chairs, and there were
crowds and crowds of people. We were playing along, going like sixty, as our
band started to pass in front of the hotel. Suddenly I saw one of the
waiters shoot up in the air, pointing with his arm, and through all this
noise I can hear him scream, "O PROFESSOR!" So the head waiter found out why
I wasn't able to be there that evening to see the competition -- I was in
it!
The next day I saw a lady I knew from meeting her on the beach all the
time, who had an apartment overlooking the Avenida. She had some friends
over to watch the parade of the samba schools, and when we went by, one of
her friends exclaimed, "Listen to that guy play the frigideira -- he is
good!" I had succeeded. I got a kick out of succeeding at something I wasn't
supposed to be able to do.
When the time came for Carnaval, not very many people from our school
showed up. There were some special costumes that were made just for the
occasion, but not enough people. Maybe they had the attitude that we
couldn't win against the really big samba schools from the city; I don't
know. I thought we were working day after day, practicing and marching for
the Carnaval, but when Carnaval came, a lot of the band didn't show up, and
we didn't compete very well. Even as we were marching around in the street,
some of the band wandered off. Funny result! I never did understand it very
well, but maybe the main excitement and fun was trying to win the contest of
the beaches, where most people felt their level was. And we did win, by the
way.

### JanSz — 2021-04-04T19:00:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-296883>

> [John Schumacher said:](/goto/post?id=296880)
>
> Happy Easter
> Our sunrise service was amazing!

[https://thirdhour.org/blog/life/holidays/easter/christ-was-crucified-thursday/](https://thirdhour.org/blog/life/holidays/easter/christ-was-crucified-thursday/)

**Christ was Crucified on Thursday**
Ours isn’t the only Christian church to post a schedule of the final week of Christ’s life during the Easter season. Knowing that the Jewish Sabbath is on Saturday and that Christ’s body was taken down from the cross because the Sabbath was nigh, Christians have assumed that He was crucified on a Friday. The normal Jewish Sabbath begins at sundown Friday night.

However, it takes more research into and experience with Judaism to truly visualize how sabbath days line up during Jewish holidays. As a Jewish convert to the Church who lived in Israel for 8 years, I have added to my experience…and to my research. During one Jewish holiday in Israel (Rosh HaShanah) we celebrated three sabbath days in a row.

****High Holy Days and Sabbaths****
High Holy Days in Judaism are all Sabbaths. If a High Holy Day falls on a Friday or Sunday, Jews celebrate two Sabbath days in a row, and since Rosh HaShanah (Jewish civil new year, or the Feast of Trumpets) is a two-day holy festival in Israel, there can be three sabbath days in a row. The year Christ died there were two Sabbath days in a row: the first day of Passover—Thursday evening to Friday evening, and the regular Sabbath—Friday evening to Saturday evening.

John the Beloved said, “The Jews therefore, because it was the _preparation_, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (_for that sabbath day was an high day_,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away” (John 19:31).

The “preparation” was for the Passover. Scholars of the Center for Nazarene Judaism have determined that there were no Saturday Passovers during the 20 years surrounding Christ’s crucifixion.

****The Shape of the Passover Week****
While there was a temple in Jerusalem, Jews were expected to make 3 pilgrimages to the temple each year to celebrate 7 highly symbolic holidays. In the spring, Jews would make their pilgrimage to offer sacrifices and to celebrate 3 festivals: Passover, the Feast of Unleavened Bread, and “Bikkurim”—the “honored son” first fruits offering.

Christ created the Law of Moses as a schoolmaster law to testify of Him and to teach the principles of sacrifice and repentance—both foundational, Aaronic Priesthood principles. In the wilderness, He dictated the holy holidays and their patterns except for the Passover, which He established just before the Israelites left Egypt.

In preparation for the Exodus, the Lord moved the religious new year from the fall to the spring month of _Nisan_ (also called _Aviv_). He scheduled the Passover offering for the 14th of Nisan. The Israelites were to take a baby lamb into each household on the 10th, so they would become attached to it and so that sacrificing it would be an act of sorrow. They sacrificed the lambs on the 14th and painted lamb’s blood on the lintels of their doorways to protect them from the destroying angel.

On the 15th of Nisan, the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Israelites left Egypt. Because of their haste, their loaves did not have time to rise, and their bread was unleavened. Since the Feast of Unleavened Bread lasts one week, we now have an 8-day holiday.

There was one more holy day commanded by the Lord. He said to celebrate it on “the day after the Sabbath that falls during Passover/Feast of Unleavened Bread.” Do you see why this would result in thousands of years of arguing among Jewish scholars? Did the Lord mean the day after the normal Sabbath, or the day after the High Holy Day Sabbath? Jews still don’t agree, so they schedule this minor observance for the 16th of Nisan.

****The Special Message of Bikkurim****
If the Lord meant the day after the normal Sabbath, then Bikkurim would always fall on a Sunday. Preparations for Bikkurim began weeks before it occurred. The priests would plant a small field of barley close to the temple. They were not allowed by the Lord to water, weed, or care for this barley at all—it had to “grow up unto itself.”

As the Jews gathered in Jerusalem to sacrifice their Paschal lambs, they brought barley with them, but it was from the previous year’s harvest. Although there had been a new barley harvest in the spring, they couldn’t eat from it until the Bikkurim offering was performed.

The high priest would go down into the field surrounded by interested onlookers, who would give their common consent when the priest found the most perfect sheaf of grain. He would set this sheaf apart by tying it with a flaxen cord. Then he would harvest it and take it to the temple to offer as a wave offering. After that wave offering, the Jews could partake of their new barley harvest. The wave offering guaranteed a fine harvest until Passover the following year.

This wave offering was offered as Christ rose from the dead on Sunday, during the Feast of Unleavened Bread. This was also the Sunday that Elijah visited Joseph Smith and Oliver Cowdery in the Kirtland Temple to bestow the sealing power. Christ instituted the resurrection, the harvest of souls unto immortality, and the sealing power enables another kind of harvest, a harvest of eternal families.

Following Christ to the Cross
So, Christ shared a Passover feast with His apostles on Wednesday night, when He established the symbolism of the sacrament. Why did He celebrate one night earlier than everyone else? When Christ commanded the entire 3-festival Passover celebration, it was to last 8 days, but by the time He was born, the Jews had contracted it to 7 days. Christ, His disciples, and the Essenes followed the old pattern.

Christ left that Wednesday Passover feast to suffer in Gethsemane and then to be betrayed and arrested. He was thrust before Herod once and Pilate twice, flogged and mocked. He was crucified on Thursday afternoon. Thus, Christ was crucified at the same time as most of the lambs in the temple were sacrificed. He was taken down from the cross before sundown on Thursday for the High Holy first day of Passover Week. He was in the tomb a full three days and three nights. He rose the morning of Bikkurim, the Honored Son wave offering.

****Richard K. Scott wrote a paper showing that Christ was crucified on Thursday using the Book of Mormon account. [You can find it in the Perks section of ThirdHour.](https://thirdhour.org/wp-content/uploads/2019/04/RichardKScottPaper.pdf)****

### John Schumacher — 2021-04-04T19:32:51+0300

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=296883)
>
> [https://thirdhour.org/blog/life/holidays/easter/christ-was-crucified-thursday/](https://thirdhour.org/blog/life/holidays/easter/christ-was-crucified-thursday/)
>
> **Christ was Crucified on Thursday**
> Ours isn’t the only Christian church to post a schedule of the final week of Christ’s life during the Easter season. Knowing that the Jewish Sabbath is on Saturday and that Christ’s body was taken down from the cross because the Sabbath was nigh, Christians have assumed that He was crucified on a Friday. The normal Jewish Sabbath begins at sundown Friday night.
>
> However, it takes more research into and experience with Judaism to truly visualize how sabbath days line up during Jewish holidays. As a Jewish convert to the Church who lived in Israel for 8 years, I have added to my experience…and to my research. During one Jewish holiday in Israel (Rosh HaShanah) we celebrated three sabbath days in a row.
>
> ****High Holy Days and Sabbaths****
> High Holy Days in Judaism are all Sabbaths. If a High Holy Day falls on a Friday or Sunday, Jews celebrate two Sabbath days in a row, and since Rosh HaShanah (Jewish civil new year, or the Feast of Trumpets) is a two-day holy festival in Israel, there can be three sabbath days in a row. The year Christ died there were two Sabbath days in a row: the first day of Passover—Thursday evening to Friday evening, and the regular Sabbath—Friday evening to Saturday evening.
>
> John the Beloved said, “The Jews therefore, because it was the _preparation_, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, (_for that sabbath day was an high day_,) besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away” (John 19:31).
>
> The “preparation” was for the Passover. Scholars of the Center for Nazarene Judaism have determined that there were no Saturday Passovers during the 20 years surrounding Christ’s crucifixion.
>
> ****The Shape of the Passover Week****
> While there was a temple in Jerusalem, Jews were expected to make 3 pilgrimages to the temple each year to celebrate 7 highly symbolic holidays. In the spring, Jews would make their pilgrimage to offer sacrifices and to celebrate 3 festivals: Passover, the Feast of Unleavened Bread, and “Bikkurim”—the “honored son” first fruits offering.
>
> Christ created the Law of Moses as a schoolmaster law to testify of Him and to teach the principles of sacrifice and repentance—both foundational, Aaronic Priesthood principles. In the wilderness, He dictated the holy holidays and their patterns except for the Passover, which He established just before the Israelites left Egypt.
>
> In preparation for the Exodus, the Lord moved the religious new year from the fall to the spring month of _Nisan_ (also called _Aviv_). He scheduled the Passover offering for the 14th of Nisan. The Israelites were to take a baby lamb into each household on the 10th, so they would become attached to it and so that sacrificing it would be an act of sorrow. They sacrificed the lambs on the 14th and painted lamb’s blood on the lintels of their doorways to protect them from the destroying angel.
>
> On the 15th of Nisan, the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Israelites left Egypt. Because of their haste, their loaves did not have time to rise, and their bread was unleavened. Since the Feast of Unleavened Bread lasts one week, we now have an 8-day holiday.
>
> There was one more holy day commanded by the Lord. He said to celebrate it on “the day after the Sabbath that falls during Passover/Feast of Unleavened Bread.” Do you see why this would result in thousands of years of arguing among Jewish scholars? Did the Lord mean the day after the normal Sabbath, or the day after the High Holy Day Sabbath? Jews still don’t agree, so they schedule this minor observance for the 16th of Nisan.
>
> ****The Special Message of Bikkurim****
> If the Lord meant the day after the normal Sabbath, then Bikkurim would always fall on a Sunday. Preparations for Bikkurim began weeks before it occurred. The priests would plant a small field of barley close to the temple. They were not allowed by the Lord to water, weed, or care for this barley at all—it had to “grow up unto itself.”
>
> As the Jews gathered in Jerusalem to sacrifice their Paschal lambs, they brought barley with them, but it was from the previous year’s harvest. Although there had been a new barley harvest in the spring, they couldn’t eat from it until the Bikkurim offering was performed.
>
>
> The high priest would go down into the field surrounded by interested onlookers, who would give their common consent when the priest found the most perfect sheaf of grain. He would set this sheaf apart by tying it with a flaxen cord. Then he would harvest it and take it to the temple to offer as a wave offering. After that wave offering, the Jews could partake of their new barley harvest. The wave offering guaranteed a fine harvest until Passover the following year.
>

> This wave offering was offered as Christ rose from the dead on Sunday, during the Feast of Unleavened Bread. This was also the Sunday that Elijah visited Joseph Smith and Oliver Cowdery in the Kirtland Temple to bestow the sealing power. Christ instituted the resurrection, the harvest of souls unto immortality, and the sealing power enables another kind of harvest, a harvest of eternal families.
>
> Following Christ to the Cross
> So, Christ shared a Passover feast with His apostles on Wednesday night, when He established the symbolism of the sacrament. Why did He celebrate one night earlier than everyone else? When Christ commanded the entire 3-festival Passover celebration, it was to last 8 days, but by the time He was born, the Jews had contracted it to 7 days. Christ, His disciples, and the Essenes followed the old pattern.
>
> Christ left that Wednesday Passover feast to suffer in Gethsemane and then to be betrayed and arrested. He was thrust before Herod once and Pilate twice, flogged and mocked. He was crucified on Thursday afternoon. Thus, Christ was crucified at the same time as most of the lambs in the temple were sacrificed. The “particle” spin in the article mentioned above believes the "electron" spin is occurring randomly. However, inside an atom there are three spins - in fact a double helix set of spins:

-   Centrifugal vortex
-   Centripetal vortex
-   Spatial circular reciprocating vortex

Probably the easiest way of thinking of it as spins in Space interacting with spins in CounterSpace.

Hey Janz,
I have experimented with lots of worm bins over the years. I prefer just a large compost pile. It allows the worms to go where they want to find best temp, moisture and food. I used to pick up bags of coffee grounds from starbucks. 5 to 10 bags per day. Thousands of pounds per year. Coffee grounds have a great C/N ratio and don't rot or attract pests. When I started, I asked the local worm farmer if they would eat onions or jalapeno. He just said he feeds them cow shit so shouldn't matter.
Interestingly, worms, like humans, digest their food with their biome. The goodness they bring to our soil,garden, and ecosystem is microbial diversity more than just cycling nutritive minerals.
Always keep your worms and compost moist as the bacteria need it. Covering the pile with straw blocks the sun, insulates, keeps in moisture and give a great place for laying worm eggs. Good luck!

I eat vegetables; so the scraps, peels, etc. go into a [https://www.nutribullet.com/shop/blenders/nutribullet-600/](https://www.nutribullet.com/shop/blenders/nutribullet-600/) at the end of each day.
Worms don't have mouths, teeth, etc. So the smoothy for the worms are easily digestible for them.
Over time, the bottom trays become "dirt" for the garden.

### JanSz — 2021-04-30T18:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-cortisol-levels.14194/post-297999>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
**Why I do not see more discussions about the fact light does not go in the straight line?**

[https://www.thunderbolts.info/wp/2014/11/18/anything-goes-2/](https://www.thunderbolts.info/wp/2014/11/18/anything-goes-2/)

**Science advances through irrational methods.**

“I was bold in the pursuit of knowledge, never fearing to follow truth and reason to whatever results they led, and bearding every authority which stood in their way.”

— Thomas Jefferson

It is a common perception that “we stand on the shoulders of giants”: that is, new ideas are based on those inherited from older investigations. If that is the case, then there is a serious hinderance inherent in the approach.

The title of this article is borrowed from Paul Feyerabend, a self-described “epistemological anarchist”, who promulgated an irreverent view of science. It is necessary, in his opinion, to promote inconsistency in the scientific method. By demanding conformity to older theories, those older theories are protected from change, thus inhibiting amended thought. That dogmatic stipulation is familiar to Electric Universe advocates when they attempt to introduce the idea that electricity governs the cosmos and not gravity, alone. The “old guard” immediately mans their guns, aiming to blow apart any intruders attempting to storm their institutionalized walls.

As Cervantes put in the mouth of Don Quijote de la Mancha: “Facts are the enemy of truth”. “Facts” are those data points born from observations that trusted in past theories. Those observations become the “rules” under which all new research is governed. They are the “laws of physics”, for example, that must not be violated lest “thought criminal” be branded on the forehead of the transgressor. The “hallowed halls” of science become the home of sanctified knowledge that brooks no denial.

It is forgotten that the worshipful dictum is an assumption. It is assumed that the charge on the electron does not vary. It is assumed that a kilogram is a kilogram no matter where or when it is measured. It is assumed that the fine structure constant is a _constant_ throughout the Universe. However, it is demonstrated time and again that those assumptions could be wrong. The gravitational constant seems to change every time it is measured. **The speed of light—considered to be one of the most inviolate cosmological statutes—has been exceeded in some laboratory experiments.** If those facts are obviated by new observations, then laws become mere suggestions.

Skepticism has been lost in modern science. To be a “skeptic” today means to attack new ideas; to marginalize the opposition with a coordinated offensive designed to eliminate competitive viewpoints. It is usually couched in a variety of logical fallacies: appeal to authority; denigration of personality; arguing adverse consequences; or demanding adherence to tradition. As the old gospel song fervently asserts, “If it was good for Paul and Silas, it’s good enough for me”.

Without an inner context, observations can be invisible. That inner context is called a theory. It can be shown that the theories that changed the models science uses to comprehend observations were often based on unsubstantiated particulars. The Electric Universe promotes theories of reality that await confirmation. In conventional conclaves, those theories are nothing but “pseudoscience”, meaning “not genuine”; not “authentic”. So-called “authentic science” is, in reality, the established dogma against which leading-edge visualization should be opposed.

Philosopher Thomas Kuhn wrote: “Examining the record of past research from the vantage of contemporary historiography, the historian of science may be tempted to exclaim that when paradigms change, the world itself changes with them. Led by a new paradigm, scientists adopt new instruments and look in new places.”

Those new paradigms do not come about because research builds on top of research stone-by-stone. Rather, those “paradigm shifts” usually come about by force. The fall of the Berlin Wall is an equivalent phenomenon. When the decision is made, nothing can withstand it, and that decision can arise suddenly.

The illogical and the unreasonable must be allowed in science, otherwise the risk is that inquiry will become compliance, and insight will never express its discoveries.
